runners.name: Notable Speech
runners.spotlight: High-class miler domestically and internationally, predominantly on good and quicker ground in accordance with his bright turn of foot; readily justified strong market support in the Lockinge at Newbury most recently, registering a fifth success at the top level and looking better than ever; bit to prove at Royal Ascot, having flopped badly in the 2024 St James's Palace Stakes and finished only fourth in this race last year; otherwise a leading player on the figures.
runners.comment: High class; better than ever in the Lockinge last time, scoring readily; leading player

runners.name: Opera Ballo
runners.spotlight: Made all for 3l success in a particularly good edition of the bet365 Mile (Group 2) at Sandown most recently, defying a 5lb penalty for his Group 1 win at Meydan and taking record to 7-9, as well as reversing Newmarket autumn placings with Zeus Olympios; doesn't necessarily need to dominate up front; new rating puts him on a par with stablemate Notable Speech and even bigger figures may be on the way; respected.
runners.comment: Clearcut win in Sandown Group 2 took his record to 7-9; very progressive; respected

runners.name: More Thunder
runners.spotlight: Usually held up in rear; did very well over 6f/7f last term, including summer wins in a major handicap and Group 2, as well as nearly landing the Wokingham at this meeting; made a cracking first attempt at 1m when only 2l behind Notable Speech (who got first run) in the Lockinge at Newbury on reappearance, taking form figures for current yard to 1121142 and suggesting he's set for another productive campaign; plausibly still improving and promises to be suited by this return to a stiffer course; major contender.
runners.comment: Good effort in the Lockinge (first 1m attempt) took his record for current yard to 1121142

runners.name: Damysus
runners.spotlight: Record is 4-8, the most recent success gained comfortably in Newmarket Group 3 on seasonal debut when completing a 1m2f/1m1f hat-trick; disappointing in the Lockinge (1m) since, not looking entirely beaten by the drop back in trip, but he's difficult to write off having been broadly progressive otherwise; rebound is possible.
runners.comment: Disappointing in the Lockinge but broadly progressive otherwise; rebound is possible

runners.name: First Conquest
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign by finishing third in 1m1f contests won by Damysus and stablemate Opera Ballo; consistent but appears very unlikely to reverse placings with those rivals and is the only runner who lacks recent match practice, being the sole seasonal debutant in the field; drops back to 1m for first time since winning a good handicap in July 2024; one of three runners for his yard and possibly in here as a pacemaker.
runners.comment: Consistent but lacks recent match practice; the only seasonal debutant in the field

runners.name: Zeus Olympios
runners.spotlight: 4-4 in his first season last year, culminating in Group 2 win at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) where he won going away from Opera Ballo; unable to confirm placings with that opponent at Sandown on reappearance but wasn't disgraced and he subsequently ran better, behind Notable Speech and More Thunder, in the Lockinge at Newbury; may yet tap back into his tremendous earlier progress and prove that he can still become a top miler; remains of major interest.
runners.comment: 4-4 last year; creditable third in the Lockinge; may yet tap back into his 3yo progress
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Cicero's Gift
runners.spotlight: Returns to the scene of his biggest performance but that 100-1 success in last year's Queen Elizabeth II Stakes over C&D (good) still seems something of a fluke, having been seemingly helped by the draw; has failed to back up the form in races won by Opera Ballo and Notable Speech this season, beaten about 7l both times; dubious claims.
runners.comment: 100-1 win in last year's QEII over C&D still seems something of fluke; dubious claims

runners.name: Docklands
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; admirable campaigner whose Listed success at Doncaster in March is flanked by respectable efforts in Group 1 events at Sha Tin; his excellent record over this C&D reads 11322214 and features two wins at Royal Ascot, including narrowly in this contest last term despite rider dropping whip over 1f out (played second fiddle to Charyn, the year's champion miler, in the 2024 edition); solid claims returned to this scenario.
runners.comment: Excellent record (11322214) over C&D features a win in this race last year; solid claims

runners.name: Ten Bob Tony
runners.spotlight: Acts particularly well on ground softer than good; very useful 7f performer who prevailed narrowly at Epsom ten days ago for a second success in the Group 3 that was formerly the John of Gaunt Stakes and run at Haydock; ran creditably over 1m in 2024 German 2,000 Guineas but this is a stiffer test at the trip and a stiffer assignment to boot.
runners.comment: Very useful over 7f; won narrowly at Epsom ten days ago; stiff 1m presents a question mark

runners.name: Confucius
runners.spotlight: Superbly bred colt (by No Nay Never out of the Cheveley Park winner Millisle) who cost 1,700,000gns as a yearling; went down narrowly on debut before readily landing the odds in maiden at Naas (6f, good) in a fast time; he's the choice of Ryan Moore and Aidan O'Brien has won this race in two of the last three years; big chance.
runners.comment: Superbly bred; ready winner at Naas; represents powerful connections; big chance

runners.name: Great Barrier Reef
runners.spotlight: Hammered six rivals at the Curragh on debut (6f, soft) and overcame residual greenness to follow up in Group 3 over the same C&D (good) three weeks ago, leading inside final 1f; Ryan Moore jumps ship to ride Confucius, but this one tops the pile on ratings and merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Two Curragh wins include Group 3; tops the pile on ratings; merits serious consideration

runners.name: Adaay Of Scarlett
runners.spotlight: Lost unbeaten record in Listed race at Sandown three weeks ago but only after a good tussle; previous 5f wins included one here, and he shapes as if he'll stay this extra furlong, but he needs to improve for it.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins include one here; should stay 6f but needs to improve for it

runners.name: Cut A Dash
runners.spotlight: Created favourable impression when beating five opponents in convincing style at York's Dante meeting (5f, good; 11-4), readily asserting from over 1f out; bred to be at least as effective over this longer trip; the pick of the British-trained runners on form, open to progress and should go well.
runners.comment: Convincing winner at York's Dante meeting; the pick of the British-trained runners on form

runners.name: God Given Talent
runners.spotlight: 28-1 for debut at Newbury (6f, good) but made a successful start, never far away and leading inside the final 1f; that race has yet to throw up any other winners but he's clearly a promising sort and he could make his presence felt.
runners.comment: 28-1 winner on debut at Newbury; promising sort who could make his presence felt

runners.name: Night In Vegas
runners.spotlight: Has made the perfect start to his career, winning readily last month over C&D (good) and at Goodwood (good to soft); those performances put him near the top of the pecking order on ratings and his stable landed 2yo events at Royal Ascot in 2021 and 2025; commands major respect.
runners.comment: 2-2, including over C&D; near the top of the list on ratings; commands major respect

runners.name: High King
runners.spotlight: Green on debut but clearly derived plenty of benefit from the experience and won in clearcut fashion at Fairyhouse (6f, good) later in May; he'll need more progress to make an impact in this higher grade but it's possible.
runners.comment: Green on debut; clearcut Fairyhouse winner next time; more needed here, but not ruled out

runners.name: Mrair
runners.spotlight: £880,000 breeze-up purchase; turned over at short odds on debut but made amends at Lingfield (6f, good) two weeks ago with plenty in hand from Final Objective despite racing freely; hard to pin down and not to be passed over lightly, with Oisin Murphy (3-8 for the stable this year) a good booking.
runners.comment: £880,000 breeze-up buy; easy Lingfield winner; hard to pin down; not passed over lightly

runners.name: Jaan Ki Tukri
runners.spotlight: Beaten three times, including when 4-7 at Bath (5f, good to firm) last month; yard landed the Coventry in 2020 with 150-1 shot Nando Parrado, another son of Kodiac; a win for this colt would be similarly surprising.
runners.comment: Beaten three times, including when at 4-7 at Bath; would be a surprise winner

runners.name: The Scallionator
runners.spotlight: Progressing fast and won going away at Listowel (about 6f, good) at the end of May; much more is needed in this higher grade but further improvement seems likely and Wesley Joyce has chosen him ahead of Arizona Raider.
runners.comment: Progressive and won well at Listowel; Wesley Joyce has chosen him ahead of Arizona Raider

runners.name: Arizona Raider
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each of his three outings and showed grit to come out on top at Roscommon (7.4f, good to yielding) four weeks ago; however, he's in much deeper here and the drop back in distance is not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Showed grit to come out on top at Roscommon but this shorter trip is not sure to suit

runners.name: Bull Shark
runners.spotlight: Well up to winning a race of some sort but he was put firmly in his place by both Great Barrier Reef (at the Curragh) and Confucius (at Naas) in the spring; again likely to come up short.
runners.comment: Well beaten by both Great Barrier Reef and Confucius; again likely to come up short

runners.name: Ruler's Pride
runners.spotlight: 550,000gns breeze-up buy; had only two opponents when scooting up at Ayr (5f, good to firm; 30-100) four weeks ago, but could hardly have been more impressive and the runner-up won the Hilary Needler at Beverley next time; speedily bred but ought to be just as effective over this extra furlong; comes from a yard with deep pool of 2yo talent, and he could be anything.
runners.comment: 550,000gns breeze-up buy; form of easy Ayr success has been boosted; could be anything

runners.name: Royal Heritage
runners.spotlight: Changed hands for 800,000euros at the breeze-ups and was strong in the market when making a winning start at Hamilton (6f, good to soft; 30-100) last Wednesday, readily pulling clear of four opponents; that form is untested and he's in much deeper here, but it's interesting that James Doyle prefers him to Ruler's Pride.
runners.comment: Bought for 800,000euros at breeze-ups; easy Hamilton winner; James Doyle's selected mount

runners.name: Siouxperb
runners.spotlight: Bred to be useful and made an impressive winning start at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm; 4-6) in April, soon in front and surging clear from over 1f out; the runner-up is 2-2 since, giving the form a solid look; stable won this race with the top-class Bradsell in 2022; has to be respected.
runners.comment: Landed the odds at Yarmouth in April and the runner-up is 2-2 since; much respected

runners.name: Easy Answer
runners.spotlight: Some promise when third of eight at Yarmouth (6f, good; 11-1) but that form looks nothing special; would need significant improvement to play a part.
runners.comment: Some promise when third at Yarmouth but would need significant improvement to play a part

runners.name: The Harv
runners.spotlight: £260,000 breeze-up buy; overcame adversity (ducked right start, hit on nose by rival jockey's whip inside final 1f) to make winning debut at Goodwood (6f, soft; 5-6) nine days ago; this is a quick turnaround but he clearly has potential.
runners.comment: £260,000 breeze-up buy; overcame adversity to win at Goodwood nine days ago; has potential

runners.name: Final Objective
runners.spotlight: Raced freely on debut at Lingfield (6f, good; 11-1) despite being hooded, but nonetheless rallied to finish second; should improve but it's hard to see him turning the tables with Mrair, the clearcut winner that day.
runners.comment: Second on debut at Lingfield; hard to see him turning tables with clearcut winner Mrair

runners.name: The Ginger Kid
runners.spotlight: 110,000euros purchase as a yearling; could not justify strong support on debut but much more clued up at Nottingham (6f, good) next time, quickening nicely to beat Cilician; further improvement possible but necessary.
runners.comment: Showed benefit of debut when winning at Nottingham; more progress possible but necessary

runners.name: Cilician
runners.spotlight: Chased home The Ginger Kid on debut at Nottingham (6f, good; 25-1) two weeks ago, finishing strongly; has plenty to find on the figures, but so did Rashabar who struck at 80-1 in this race in 2024 for the same connections.
runners.comment: Chased home The Ginger Kid at Nottingham; stable won this race with an 80-1 shot in 2024

runners.name: Treasurer
runners.spotlight: Shaped better than the bare result suggests when fourth of 11 at Nottingham (6f, good; 11-1; kept on well after meeting trouble) 16 days ago, and that race is already working out well; however, this is much tougher and he's the Amo Racing second string on jockey bookings.
runners.comment: Shaped better than bare result when fourth at Nottingham, but this is much tougher

runners.name: Kamaal
runners.spotlight: Raced freely and faded when fifth to God Given Talent at Newbury (6f, good; 10-1) last month; bred to be useful and could improve markedly, but he's undoubtedly one of the outsiders.
runners.comment: Fifth to God Given Talent at Newbury; could improve but he's one of the outsiders

runners.name: Big Mojo
runners.spotlight: 6f winner here last April; best form at that trip in Group 1s since, going down narrowly in the July Cup at Newmarket (good to firm) and later winning the Sprint Cup at Haydock (good) last September; beaten favourite at Goodwood (good to soft) in between when having a rare 5f run but this stiffer test at the trip and quicker ground will suit; needs extra all the same.
runners.comment: Best form in Group 1s over 6f, winning one at Haydock; having a rare run at 5f

runners.name: Asfoora
runners.spotlight: Superb Australian mare who was produced to perfection to win this in 2024 (good to firm); her preparation didn't go well last year when only fifth but she later added two more Group 1 wins in the Nunthorpe at York and the Abbaye at Longchamp; well below best in two runs this year (without regular blinkers which now return with tongue-tie added) but the wet winter in Britain would have been a shock to her system and she's not at all ruled out.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024; two more Group 1 wins since; below best this year but blinkers return

runners.name: Time For Sandals
runners.spotlight: Her win in the Group 1 Commonwealth Cup (good to firm) at the Royal meeting last year was a big advance on previous form and, although she ran perfectly well back over 5f next time, when a close third to Jm Jungle in a Group 2 at Goodwood (good to soft), her overall form suggests she'll be found wanting on her first 5f run since.
runners.comment: Has 5f form at Group 2 level; best to win 6f Group 1 Commonwealth Cup (6f) here in 2025
runners.owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan

runners.name: Rayevka
runners.spotlight: Ran a scorcher when coming from well back to finish third of 21 to Time For Sandals in the Group 1 Commonwealth Cup here (6f, good to firm) last June; two defeats at Meydan this spring but she was impressive back over 5f in a Group 3 at Longchamp (soft) in May and this up-and-comer is expected by connections to improve for the return to quick ground; much respected.
runners.comment: Handles all ground; good 3rd in Group 1 over 6f here last June; impressive at 5f latest

runners.name: Night Raider
runners.spotlight: Turned his back on a brilliant AW career and, after some fruitless efforts on turf in 2025, as when fading into mid-division in this race, the switch has paid off handsomely since he was gelded, the usual front-running tactics working well at Newmarket (good to firm) before he showed he doesn't have to lead when arriving late for a cosy Group 2 win at Haydock (also 5f, good to soft) in May; this stiff 5f asks a question but he could achieve yet more.
runners.comment: Brilliant on AW; turf success hard to come by until gelded and won both 5f starts since

runners.name: American Affair
runners.spotlight: A massive improver in 2025 as he was still cleaning up in handicaps in the spring before nipping home to win this race (good to firm) from Frost At Dawn; down the field on first run since, at York in May, but he came on a ton to chase home Night Raider at Haydock (5f, good to soft) latest; likely to pull out the stops for another excellent effort.
runners.comment: Massive improver in 2025 who won this on final start of the year; coming to the boil again

runners.name: Frost At Dawn
runners.spotlight: Has only ever won three races but there's no doubting how smart she is when getting good to firm ground, as when beaten by only American Affair in the 2025 running and a later third to Asfoora in the Group 1 Nunthorpe at York; it's easy to excuse two defeats in 2026 and look forward to another solid show.
runners.comment: Well suited by good to firm, as when 2nd in the 2025 running and 3rd in Group 1 at York

runners.name: Aspect Island
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo form when dropped to 5f on final two starts, signing off with a close third of 12 in the Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint (firm); he needed to progress as 3yo to have a chance today but that hasn't happened, so the first-time visor has a big job to do.
runners.comment: Best 2yo form when 3rd in Grade 1 in US; no progress as 3yo; first-time headgear

runners.name: First Instinct
runners.spotlight: Useful 5f form as a 3yo, inching forward again when gaining a narrow win over Shagraan in a Group 3 at Newbury (5f, good to soft; acts on good) last September; she was entitled to need the run behind Night Raider at Newmarket in April but a big advance on her form is needed.
runners.comment: Won Group 3 last September; better for comeback run but a big advance on form is needed

runners.name: Mission Central
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo form over 6f, including when winning a valuable conditions race on this track; vulnerable over 5f then but speed tests at this trip have proved his forte in 2026, disposing of his rivals in two Listed races at Naas (good) quite impressively; intriguing contender back up in class.
runners.comment: Best form over 6f as 2yo but speedy and has impressed in two 5f Listed races this year

runners.name: Behike
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin winner of both 6f starts, on Lingfield AW and in a turf novice on that course; has potential but also a mighty task trying to cross the chasm into a Group 1 against the pros on first 5f run.
runners.comment: Ready winner of both 6f starts; mighty task crossing the chasm into a Group 1; new trip

runners.name: Getreadytorumble
runners.spotlight: Unraced as 2yo; made good progress in 2025, ending with a Listed third of ten over C&D (soft) but, after a satisfactory return in a similar race at Naas (good) in April, he was never involved in the Group 2 at Haydock won by Night Raider; aiming high today.
runners.comment: Good progress as 3yo, finishing 3rd in C&D Listed race on final start; aiming high

runners.name: Overpass
runners.spotlight: The latest in a long line of terrific Australian sprinters to grace this meeting; winless since impressing from the widest draw in a Group 1 at his native Ascot in late 2024 but he's run many fine races since, including in the Group 1 Everest (6f, good) last October when having second place taken from him only near the line; that would have been strong form even without the winner Ka Ying Rising, the world's top sprinter, and he should have the speed to hold his usual prominent position back over 5f; solid chance.
runners.comment: Australian; races prominently; many fine efforts in home country; good chance back at 5f

runners.name: Shagraan
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 5f Listed wins at Beverley (good to firm) and over C&D (soft) either side of a Group 2 second to First Instinct at Newbury; has a lot to find on those efforts and also on this year's runs for a new yard.
runners.comment: Latest two wins at Listed level; has much to find on that form and on this year's efforts

runners.name: Monteille
runners.spotlight: Admirable close eighth of 20 in this last June (good to firm) considering she's well suited by good to soft or slower ground; showed her best colours when beating Rayevka to win Group 3 at Longchamp (5f, very soft) last September but will do well to improve on last year's run under these conditions.
runners.comment: Close 8th in this race last year but suited by very soft when Group 3 winner in September

runners.name: Rosy Affair
runners.spotlight: Successful over 5f in the very early days but three wins in her last four starts have all been over 6f, in Listed races at home and a Group 3 at Deauville; returns to this trip for the first time since beaten in a Listed event last July and others have much stronger claims.
runners.comment: Won three of last four starts at 6f; needs to find lots more in a Group 1 back over 5f

runners.name: Jm Jungle
runners.spotlight: Best form on sharp tracks; never better than for a Group 2 win at Goodwood (5f, good to soft) last August but well held in the Nunthorpe at York next time on his sole attempt at a Group 1 in Britain; has work to do.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner over 5f last August; well held in sole Group 1 in Britain; up against it
runners.owner: MPS Racing Ltd & The Ayrshire Tradesmen

runners.name: Heavenly Heather
runners.spotlight: Useful handicapper whose three wins have all come on the AW, most recently over 5f in January; faces a daunting task this time.
runners.comment: Useful handicapper whose 3 wins have come on the AW, the latest in January; daunting task

runners.name: Jakajaro
runners.spotlight: 3-30 turf strike-rate doesn't do justice to his ability and he confirmed his recent improvement when a highly creditable fourth, again from the front, in a Group 2 at Haydock (good to soft; acts well on good) in May; this stiffer track and tougher opposition are likely to count against him.
runners.comment: Creditable 4th from the front in recent Group 2 over 5f; stiffer track and opposition here

runners.name: Azure Angel
runners.spotlight: Improved form when winning a 5f Listed race at Bath (good) in April; finished a creditable fifth of eight in a similar Carlisle race (good to firm) since but she's up a gum tree at this level.
runners.comment: Improved to win 5f Listed race in April; held at that level since; much more needed

runners.name: Ain't Nobody
runners.spotlight: Won the Listed Windsor Castle at this meeting as a 2yo; some ups and downs since, with his second to Asfoora in the Group 1 Nunthorpe at York (good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) last August showing what can be done on a good day; however, he needs to rise far above two heavy defeats this year.
runners.comment: Won Listed 2yo race here in 2024; good 2nd in Group 1 last August; well below best in 2026

runners.name: Rumstar
runners.spotlight: Acts well on good or quicker; Listed winner over C&D; has had excuses for two Group 1 defeats at 5f in Britain, not ideally drawn in this race last June and checked in his run in the Nunthorpe at York in August, albeit still doing well in the latter in fifth of 17 to Asfoora; useful comeback at Newmarket in April when a close second to Night Raider; can be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Improved over 5f in 2025; a hampered 5th in Group 1 last August; good reappearance

runners.name: Cover Up
runners.spotlight: One of his best domestic efforts when winning a C&D handicap last July; seemingly improved when successful in 5f Listed and Group 2 events at Meydan (yielding and good) in midwinter but has much more to do in this company.
runners.comment: Won C&D handicap last July; Group 2 winner at Meydan since; more needed at this level

runners.name: Miss Attitude
runners.spotlight: Useful in 5f Listed races last year, finishing second at York (good to firm) in July and winning at Deauville (good) the following month, but hard to fancy on her Group-race form or 2026 efforts.
runners.comment: Useful in 5f Listed races last year but hard to fancy on Group-race form or 2026 efforts

runners.name: Mgheera
runners.spotlight: Had a brainstorm before this race last year when refusing to go in the stalls but she'd impressed the time before when winning a Group 2 at Haydock (5f, good); some respectable efforts in Group races over the last 12 months haven't suggested she'll be a serious threat this time.
runners.comment: Impressive in Group 2 at Haydock last May; has work to do on subsequent efforts
runners.owner: Lord Lloyd Webber And Arthur Mitchell

runners.name: Starlust
runners.spotlight: Smart 5f form as 3yo included close third in the Group 1 Nunthorpe at York and a win in the Breeders' Cup Turf Sprint (firm); just four runs since, best of those when fourth of 20 in this race last year when ending up away from the main action; went to stud after (that career is on hold for now) and needs his recent Carlisle reappearance to have brought him on plenty.
runners.comment: Smart 3yo form included close 3rd in Group 1; has been to stud; low-key return to action

runners.name: Bow Echo
runners.spotlight: Hugely progressive and unbeaten in his four races, all over 1m on good/firmer; impressively bagged Classic honours at Newmarket in sole start this term, earning an RPR that makes him the joint-best 2,000 Guineas winner since Frankel; the next two home - Gstaad and Distant Storm - franked the form by filling the first two places in the Irish version; sets a tall standard, accordingly, and his trajectory brings the tantalising prospect of even bigger figures; massively exciting colt.
runners.comment: The joint-best 2,000 Guineas winner since Frankel; still unbeaten; massively exciting colt
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Gstaad
runners.spotlight: Very solid performer who continues to improve steadily and has never finished out of the first two; readily landed the Coventry at this meeting 12 months ago and has performed well at Group/Grade 1 level since, most recently registering a 3l success in the Irish 2,000 Guineas at the Curragh, taking record over 1m to 2-3; clear best of the remainder behind Bow Echo at Newmarket earlier in May and should give the rematch his best shot.
runners.comment: Very solid performer; second to Bow Echo in 2,000 Guineas and landed the Irish equivalent
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Talk Of New York
runners.spotlight: Very promising colt who has a record of 3-4 overall and 2-2 since wearing a hood; above-average winner of the Heron Stakes at Sandown (1m, good; Listed) most recently, coming off strong pace set by a stablemate and storming clear to beat his closest pursuer by just over 5l; looks a Group 1 performer in the making, the way he's going, and it should be remembered that he was second-favourite in ante-post betting for the 2,000 Guineas prior to a change of plan; adds great interest.
runners.comment: Very promising; stormed clear in Listed Heron Stakes most recently; adds great interest

runners.name: Puerto Rico
runners.spotlight: Stablemate of Gstaad; appears versatile as regards ground; tough colt who proved a useful maiden prior to ending last season with a hat-trick when upped to 7f/1m in major 2yo races, including two French Group 1s; creditable fourth when favourite for the Poulains at Longchamp on reappearance; has significant form, may rate higher still over this distance and looks set to have his own way up front (gained his wins making all); one of the main players.
runners.comment: Made all in three major 2yo races; creditable fourth in the Poulains on reappearance
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Power Blue
runners.spotlight: Gained his 2yo Group 1 success over 6f then ran respectably over 7f on reappearance; midfield in the 2,000 Guineas and fourth in the Irish equivalent since upped to 1m, both times finishing behind some of today's rivals and leaving the impression he ideally needs a return to shorter; likely to be outstayed and outclassed.
runners.comment: Efforts in the 2,000 Guineas and Irish equivalent suggest he needs a drop back in trip

runners.name: Lord Britain
runners.spotlight: Quite a progressive sort on AW and has gained both wins over 7f at Kempton, including in Class 4 handicap latest start; soundly beaten in both turf attempts, always behind in the 2,000 Guineas on second occasion; plenty to find and plenty to prove back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Both wins over 7f on Kempton AW; plenty to find and plenty to prove back on turf

runners.name: Bahadur
runners.spotlight: Progressive on AW last autumn and another improved effort when he went on over 3f out to win a five-runner race at Goodwood (upped to 2m, good) on latest outing; up 4lb but could have more to give as a stayer.
runners.comment: Another improved effort to win five-runner race at Goodwood (upped to 2m, good) last time
runners.owner: J William Rowley & S P C Woods

runners.name: Tim Toe
runners.spotlight: Last year he was a bumper winner (sole attempt) and ran twice over hurdles; now he's a useful prospect, at the least, under Flat rules having won a maiden at Bellewstown (11.4f, soft) and conditions event at Listowel (1m, good) and he kept on well behind Kizlyar over 1m6f in between, all from off the pace; this race is radically different but he is promising and seriously unexposed.
runners.comment: Useful prospect, at the least, after winning two of his three starts under Flat rules

runners.name: Defiantly
runners.spotlight: Unraced on firmer than good and his form claims are pretty flimsy; however, he's one of the dark horses of the race after just six starts and he's shaped at 1m6f as if he may well be an out-and-out stayer; has tried cheekpieces and a visor once each, and now blinkers are given a go; close relation Middle Earth was third over 2m in last year's Melbourne Cup.
runners.comment: Form claims are pretty flimsy but he's one of the dark horses; could be out-and-out stayer

runners.name: Puturhandstogether
runners.spotlight: Won 4yo handicap hurdle at 2025 Cheltenham Festival and took a huge prize for Irish Cesarewitch on Flat at the Curragh (2m1f, yielding) last September, in the latter bursting clear 1f out and scoring with considerably more authority than the margin suggests; underwhelming over hurdles before last month's Chester Cup, in which he also had the widest draw, but he started favourite nevertheless and made some headway from the back to finish seventh of 15; he didn't have the rub of the green that day and is shortlisted even though connections also now resort to headgear.
runners.comment: Won well in Irish Cesarewitch (2m1f, yielding) last September; excuses in the Chester Cup

runners.name: Lavender Hill Mob
runners.spotlight: 1m4f winner from the front off this mark at Newmarket last May who also scored with a prominent ride over hurdles at Cheltenham (2m5f) in November; has a bit to prove over 2m on Flat, including having been always behind on both starts this year; now tries a visor (often wears cheekpieces) and maybe there will be a change of tactics.
runners.comment: Always behind this year; bit to prove over 2m on Flat; now tries visor (often cheekpieces)

runners.name: Kizlyar
runners.spotlight: French 1m6f winner who tried hurdling for new yard last winter; tailed off in the heavy-ground handicap won by Mordor on return to Flat but dictated the pace and kept his challengers at bay in a six-runner conditions event at Killarney (1m6f, good; Tim Toe third and Defiantly fourth) five weeks ago; improvement is needed but he's still something of an unknown quantity.
runners.comment: Won a 1m6f conditions event at Killarney five weeks ago; something of an unknown quantity

runners.name: Mordor
runners.spotlight: Brought from Ireland last year for Glorious Goodwood (2m4f, soft; second off 6lb lower than today) and the Cesarewitch (2m2f, good to firm; 14th); reappeared this spring with a career best on Flat to win at Navan (1m6f, heavy) and a creditable third in 3m hurdle race, but another career high is needed.
runners.comment: 2nd at Glorious Goodwood (2m4f); in good form this year but another career high is needed

runners.name: Ismahane
runners.spotlight: Officially unraced on firmer than good; trained last year in Germany, including when runner-up in Group 3 Italian St Leger (1m7f, heavy) before changing hands for 72,000euros; well held when taking minor honours over hurdles (January and May) in her two runs for new yard, but cheekpieces (tried once last year) return and getting back to the Flat may suit.
runners.comment: Second in Italian St Leger (1m7f, heavy) last season and getting back to the Flat may suit

runners.name: Small Fry
runners.spotlight: 1m4f festival wins off much lower marks at Chester and Epsom preceded his 100,000gns purchase last July; two Flat runs (including 2m Irish Cesarewitch) last autumn didn't work out but there was encouragement to be drawn from his two hurdle races last month (second on latest) and he scored over an extended 1m6f in 2024; not entirely dismissed, from stable which runs seven.
runners.comment: Scored over an extended 1m6f in 2024 and twice at 1m4f last year; not entirely dismissed

runners.name: Annabel's Ghost
runners.spotlight: 4-22 in France, including two 2m wins and latest came with a very late bid in January (AW); peak efforts (not his last two) give him a form chance on this first start since changing hands and leaving Andre Fabre.
runners.comment: 4-22 in France, latest win with a very late bid in January (2m, AW); has left Andre Fabre

runners.name: Siempre Arturo
runners.spotlight: Wore cheekpieces last six starts, winning in late summer last year on first two occasions; a late effort took him from last to fifth of 14 when he made a good reappearance here (1m4f, good to firm) last month but venturing beyond an extended 1m4f for first time provides a huge question mark.
runners.comment: Ran on well on return but venturing beyond an extended 1m4f provides a huge question mark

runners.name: Reaching High
runners.spotlight: Dam is Ascot Gold Cup winner Estimate; inconsistent for Sir Michael Stoute in 2024; beaten on the nod at Leopardstown (1m4f) last May on debut for new yard, after which he was all the rage for this race but it was a nightmare for favourite backers, as he was shuffled back (from stall 1) and towards the rear entering straight before running on nicely until denied any room again in the last half furlong; absent since but same mark today and he is surely capable of significantly better; granted better luck, he should have a big shout.
runners.comment: All the rage off today's same mark for this race last year but had no luck; absent since
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Galileo Dame
runners.spotlight: Unraced on firmer than good; second at Navan (1m6f, heavy) in March on Flat handicap debut; backed that up with her excellent, close fourth of 15 when third favourite for the Chester Cup (2m2f, good; 1lb higher today) in May, coming from towards rear after a slow start; has to be considered but such a wide draw may mean she again has to come from well back.
runners.comment: Close fourth of 15 from towards rear in the Chester Cup (2m2f, good); 1lb higher today
runners.owner: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited

runners.name: Glenroyal
runners.spotlight: A heavy defeat over hurdles five weeks ago was his only race since October; however, he won three of his four starts once equipped with this headgear on Flat last July-October; the furthest of those wins was 1m5f and he was well beaten trying 1m6f, but it may be rash to dismiss him as a stayer, given he's brother to Magellan Strait who won the 2023 Irish Cesarewitch (2m1f) and half-brother to Lagostovegas who won this race in 2018.
runners.comment: Furthest win was over 1m5f but two siblings were stayers of note; one won this in 2018

runners.name: All In You
runners.spotlight: Ran three times on the Flat in France, winning over 1m7f on testing ground on first two occasions; that was back in 2023 and spring 2024, but he was a progressive and fairly useful hurdler in the hood for this yard last season (last seen in April) and is unexposed for this Flat handicap debut.
runners.comment: Dual 1m7f French Flat winner; a progressive and fairly useful hurdler for this yard

runners.name: Beylerbeyi
runners.spotlight: Won the Cesarewitch at Newmarket (2m2f, good to firm; 9lb higher today) last October and had some very solid AW form over 1m2f-2m this year; not nearly so good in his two races back on turf but his recent run at Epsom (1m2f) should have teed him up nicely for this; current mark and tactical issues coming from the rear are the chief worries.
runners.comment: Won the Cesarewitch at Newmarket (2m2f, good to firm; 9lb higher today) last October

runners.name: Bunting
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate (two maiden wins) in Flat and hurdling spheres and he went the wrong way in his latest hurdles campaign; however, he ran well in two major Flat handicaps last season, including when third to Beylerbeyi in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket (2m2f, good to firm); 3lb higher today but 7lb claimer Harry Vigors is booked; like so many, he has each-way hopes.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but he was third to Beylerbeyi in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket (2m2f)

runners.name: Comfort Zone
runners.spotlight: Always behind in his two races this spring (1m2f/1m4f; ended pulled up last time) and last season was a mixed bag; however, his strong-finishing thirds last year in this race (off 1lb lower than today) and another big-field contest at Naas (2m) show that he needs a close look, particularly as some headgear (absent this season) is reinstated.
runners.comment: Always behind this spring (pulled up last time) but strong-finishing 3rd in this last year

runners.name: Barnso
runners.spotlight: Markedly progressive overall when trained by Natalia Lupini in Northern Ireland, rounding it off with an always-prominent fourth of 16 at Royal Ascot 2025 (1m6f, good to firm; 2lb lower today); respectable show at Goodwood last summer, ten weeks before running poorly in the Cesarewitch when going beyond 1m6f for first time was not to blame; bit to prove on first run since.
runners.comment: 4th at Royal Ascot (1m6f) in 2025; off since running poorly in the Cesarewitch in October

runners.name: Westminster Moon
runners.spotlight: Officially unraced on firmer than good; ex-Polish and German, placed in 1m2f Group races in 2024 before a 260,000euros sale; failed to fire last year (including one 2m1f race) but shaped nicely on second start this term and landed a gamble in clearcut fashion in 24-runner handicap at the Curragh (1m6f, good) on latest, challenging 2f out; he was ridden that day as if stamina is a strength and, despite his revised mark, is one to note.
runners.comment: Landed a gamble in clearcut fashion in 24-runner race at the Curragh (1m6f, good) latest

runners.name: Survie
runners.spotlight: Ex-French mare who has several pieces of Group 1 form over middle distances, including in Saudi Arabia for this yard in February; however, she's been well below par in her two outings back in Britain this spring, albeit with 1m on AW too sharp for her on the first occasion.
runners.comment: Below par back in Britain this spring but can mix it at Group 1 level at her best

runners.name: Haatem
runners.spotlight: Landed a second win at this meeting when seeing off Galen, King's Gambit and Enfjaar in this race on good to firm 12 months ago; below-par seventh on 1m2f Goodwood reappearance 25 days ago but this has presumably been the target again; the mount of James Doyle from the three Wathnan runners.
runners.comment: Took this last year, his second Royal Ascot win; last month's reappearance likely needed

runners.name: Arabian Light
runners.spotlight: Won handicap at Meydan (1m2f, good) in January and very close second in Group 3 there (1m2f, good) later that month; returned to Britain with a creditable fourth (Wimbledon Hawkeye fifth) of six to Ombudsman in the Brigadier Gerard at Sandown (1m2f, good) 19 days ago; Buick is on him rather than Ancient Wisdom; likely to be on the premises.
runners.comment: Solid return to Britain when fourth in Brigadier Gerard; the mount of Buick from yard pair

runners.name: Galen
runners.spotlight: Consistent performer who chased home Haatem in this on good to firm last year; ended 2025 with solid efforts in Bahrain and Hong Kong and it's probably worth forgiving him a rare off day in Saudi Arabia in February; capable of making a bold bid in this again assuming stall 15 isn't too troublesome.
runners.comment: Second to Haatem in 2025; mainly reliable and can bounce back from below-par run latest
runners.owner: Barry Taylor/T G Fitzgerald/Prof Paul H Redmond/Barry Fowler

runners.name: Map Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Group 2 winner and Group 1 placed over 1m2f at Longchamp last year (also fourth in Prince Of Wales's over C&D) but he ended 2025 on a low note and has been below par in two outings over 1m4f at Longchamp this spring, markedly so last time; big shout if back to his best in first-time cheekpieces but James Doyle sticks with Haatem.
runners.comment: Good shout on his best 2025 form but hasn't fired this year; Doyle looks elsewhere

runners.name: Nahraan
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; rapidly developed into a smart performer last year, making it 3-3 when just coming out on top in the Listed Glasgow at Hamilton (1m3f) in July; lost unbeaten record when third in 1m2f Longchamp Group 3 in the autumn but the two ahead of him were Croix Du Nord and Daryz and it was another slight step forward in terms of RPRs; raced only on good/very soft on turf; a surprise if there isn't more to come from him in 2026.
runners.comment: Won first three starts, including Listed; third in Group 3 final run; surely more to come

runners.name: Wimbledon Hawkeye
runners.spotlight: Admirably consistent in defeat in Group races last year before winning the Grade 3 Nashville Derby at Kentucky (10.5f, firm) in August; well beaten in the Breeders' Cup Turf on final 2025 start but offered enough to think he retains all his ability when fifth of six to Ombudsman in Brigadier Gerard at Sandown (1m2f, good) on reappearance 19 days ago; considered.
runners.comment: Grade 3 winner in US last year; fair fifth in strong Brigadier Gerard on return

runners.name: Ancient Wisdom
runners.spotlight: No win since the 2024 Bahrain Trophy at Newmarket but runner-up in Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, good to soft; beaten a neck) and Group 3 at Newbury (1m4f, soft) last autumn; below-form 5l third in 1m4f Kempton Listed race (AW debut) in December when last seen; wears first-time cheekpieces; Buick seemingly prefers Arabian Light.
runners.comment: Second in Group 3 last autumn; below par on AW final start; Buick on Arabian Light

runners.name: Dividend
runners.spotlight: Had a very productive spell at Meydan earlier in the year, winning two handicaps before progressing again when second to Quddwah in Group 2 Singspiel and fifth to Ombudsman in Group 1 Dubai Turf (both 1m1f, good); those efforts suggest he can make his presence felt in Listed races back in Britain but this is a particularly strong one.
runners.comment: In top form in Dubai this year; capable of making presence felt at Listed level in Britain

runners.name: Enfjaar
runners.spotlight: Best effort in a truncated 2025 campaign when fourth to Haatem in this race (good to firm); showed enough to think he retains all his ability when third in 1m2f Goodwood Listed race (good) on reappearance 25 days ago; could reach the frame in this again.
runners.comment: Fourth to Haatem last year; warmed up with creditable fourth, showing he retains ability
runners.owner: T & Mrs L Standish & Mrs A Spence

runners.name: King's Gambit
runners.spotlight: No win since landing two of his first four starts but has a largely consistent record in Listed/Group company since, including third to Haatem and Galen in this race on good to firm 12 months ago; again ran well when chasing home Damysus in Group 3 at Newmarket (1m1f, good) on reappearance in April; a subsequent Chester run was disappointing but his record suggests he's unlikely to stay down for long.
runners.comment: Largely consistent in defeat in Listed/Group races, including third in this last year

runners.name: Persica
runners.spotlight: Versatile as regards ground; smart on his day and enhanced his good Epsom record when only narrowly denied a second successive win in the Group 3 Diomed (8.5f, good to soft) 11 days ago; Listed winner over this trip on good to firm in 2024; good chance on the Epsom form but stall 16 isn't ideal.
runners.comment: Went close to second successive win in 8.5f Epsom Group 3 recently; stays 1m2f; considered

runners.name: Adelaide River
runners.spotlight: Second in the Irish Derby and Grand Prix de Paris for Aidan O'Brien in 2023; failed to match that level in Australia until finishing a nose second in 1m2f Group 3 at Randwick last September; down the field in the Caulfield Cup on his final start there but resumed his European career with a pleasing staying-on third to Minnie Hauk in 1m2f Curragh Group 2 (good) six weeks ago; raced largely on good or softer on turf (below form in 2023 Derby on good to firm); his yard has had three placed in this since 2021.
runners.comment: Resumed his European career when third in 1m2f Curragh Group 2 last month

runners.name: Ghostwriter
runners.spotlight: Sold on the eve of this meeting last year for £2,000,000 and finished a creditable third to Rebel's Romance and Al Riffa in the Group 2 Hardwicke (1m4f, good to firm) a few days later; withdrawn after rearing over being saddled up on an intended start at last year's Newmarket July meeting; subsequently left Clive Cox and hasn't been seen since.
runners.comment: Off since third in last year's Hardwicke (left Clive Cox after) but player if near best

runners.name: Gaucher
runners.spotlight: Won a Grade 3 hurdle at Tipperary (good to yielding) last October and progressive form on AW since reverting to the Flat for this yard, posting two easy wins at Dundalk (1m4f/10.6f) before excellent second in 1m2f Newcastle handicap on Good Friday; not that far off on form but quickish ground would be an unknown (raced only on good or softer on turf).
runners.comment: Progressive on AW since back to Flat; not far off on form; quickish ground asks a question
runners.owner: Mrs Rose Boyd & Mrs Marie J Armstrong

runners.name: Royal Rhyme
runners.spotlight: Did well initially in his career, including when beating three rivals in the 2024 Brigadier Gerard at Sandown (1m2f, soft); 0-9 since, albeit twice placed at the highest level; pulled too hard when beaten about 6l into sixth of 12 on return in a Group 3 at the Curragh (1m2f, soft) in April; never featured at Chester in a Group 2 on good since; good to firm ground is a worry.
runners.comment: 0-9 since 2024 Brigadier Gerard win on soft and may find this ground too lively
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Gamrai
runners.spotlight: Late developer who ran once as a 2yo; the waves made last season have gathered scale in handicaps this year, as he backed up his comfortable AW win (1m3f) with a length defeat by Daiquiri Bay at Newmarket (1m4f, good to firm; led for the most part); he clearly needs to find more again (off 2lb higher) to gain his revenge on the winner but these Gosden runners have a tendency to get better and better; the stable has won this twice since 2021.
runners.comment: Second off 2lb lower latest, but Gosden runners have a tendency to get better and better

runners.name: Valiancy
runners.spotlight: Made his debut just over a year ago; firmly on the upgrade when signing off last season with a handicap win at Haydock (1m6f, good; first-time cheekpieces) and following up on his reappearance at Hamilton (1m5f, good; no headgear) where the narrow margin of victory masked his superiority (he overcame trouble); he's up a further 8lb but the improvement could keep coming; James Doyle prefers him over Wathnan's other pair; big player.
runners.comment: Good value for his Hamilton win off 8lb lower; the improvement could keep coming

runners.name: Ascending
runners.spotlight: Won the Cork Derby over 1m4f before following up in the 2m4f handicap on this card 12 months ago (good to firm) and that golden spell continued when second in the Ebor at York (1m6f); only 2lb higher than at York so he's not handicapped out of this, and last month's run in a Group 3 after 215 days away should have brought him on fitness-wise; respected.
runners.comment: Won the 2m4f race on this card last year but he's classy over various distances

runners.name: Daiquiri Bay
runners.spotlight: After just the solitary run as 2yo he made steady progress last season and delivered a clear personal best when reeling in the front-running Gamrai in a 1m4f Newmarket handicap at the Guineas meeting (good to firm; first run since gelded) on reappearance; up just 4lb and this return to a longer trip won't bother him; hard to knock.
runners.comment: Had nine races but it was his first since gelded when overcoming Gamrai at Newmarket
runners.owner: A Stennett, G Kennington & C Hodgson

runners.name: Sing Us A Song
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-9 overall and going the right way in handicaps, finishing a clear second (from the front) over 1m4f at York (good) last time behind a sharp Haggas improver whom he'd beaten over 1m6f on final run last season (good to soft; first run after gelding operation); this return to 1m6f has to be a good thing; respected.
runners.comment: Second to an improver at York and today's return to 1m6f has to be a good thing

runners.name: Stressfree
runners.spotlight: Not a prolific winner, with his waiting tactics, and never involved when 7-1 for last year's 1m4f Duke of Edinburgh at this meeting; however, it would be unwise not to recall his fourth in the Ebor at York last August (1m6f, good to firm) off a 1lb higher mark and he was unsuited by the steady pace at York last time out (1m4f); could get competitive should this pan out favourably; stable won this in 2022.
runners.comment: Hold-up performer; hard to win with but his fourth in last year's Ebor heightens interest

runners.name: Paddy The Squire
runners.spotlight: Had a ball last year after being gelded, winning three of his six starts and as good as ever when a close fifth stepped up to 1m6f at Haydock in September; however, he is a 6yo and perhaps high in the weights now, a suspicion seemingly confirmed by his York run one month ago; there's no obvious significance in the return of cheekpieces.
runners.comment: High in the weights, a suspicion seemingly confirmed by his York run a month ago

runners.name: Ernst Blofeld
runners.spotlight: His form eventually ran cold for the Gosdens last season but he's back on the up for his new yard, following his pleasing comeback at Newmarket with a decisive win at Kempton where he swept by late on (1m4f, AW); has low enough mileage to believe he'll cope fine with a 7lb rise and, despite not being Wathnan's first string on jockey arrangements, he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Impressed at Kempton and should transfer that progress back to turf

runners.name: Incensed
runners.spotlight: It wasn't a strong Listed race in which she finished third at Goodwood (1m4f, good) in May and the steady pace casts further doubts about that form; the handicapper saw fit to raise her 5lb and that spells danger in this competitive race back up in trip.
runners.comment: Went up 5lb for Listed 3rd latest and that spells danger in this competitive a handicap

runners.name: Aeronautic
runners.spotlight: Still looked a bit wet behind the ears when beaten under 4l into fifth in this 12 months ago (7-1) and has raised his game subsequently, notably when only just missing out in a 1m6f handicap at Glorious Goodwood and posting another placed effort at Meydan during the winter (1m4f, good); absent since below par in a Group 3 there in February; should have freshened up since with this presumably the plan; interesting.
runners.comment: Encouraging 5th in this last year; improvement since and returns a fresh horse

runners.name: Hallelujah U
runners.spotlight: Ran surprisingly well to finish so close in last year's Queen's Vase over C&D but perhaps flattered by that given subsequent defeats (Group 3 and a strong handicap); gelded prior to tailing off on his hurdling debut at Cheltenham in November and absent since; the return of cheekpieces is no bad thing.
runners.comment: 6th in last year's Queen's Vase; off since November when tailed off on hurdle debut

runners.name: Enemy
runners.spotlight: Won 1m4f/2m handicaps on the AW (3-36 on turf) earlier this year but this is a different ball game altogether and his run at Carlisle 17 days ago was no more than average; plenty of others appeal more.
runners.comment: His run at Carlisle 17 days ago was no more than average; plenty of others appeal more

runners.name: Green Cape
runners.spotlight: Only 1-10 for Andre Fabre, the win gained over 1m3f on good to soft last summer; has had only two races for current stable and ran fairly well in the face of a stiff task in an Abu Dhabi Listed assignment in February latest (1m4f, good; wore tongue-tie; admittedly perhaps flattered from the front); it wouldn't be a bolt from the blue should he feature in the finish.
runners.comment: Off since February but that was a respectable effort in an Abu Dhabi Listed race

runners.name: Duraji
runners.spotlight: Only 1-10 for Dermot Weld but was highly tried on occasions; made his debut for Richard Hughes in the Chester Cup (2m2f, good) and, having broken well from his wide draw, he put up a bold show from the front only to be reeled in late in the day; a 2lb nudge is easy to stomach and dropping back to 1m6f could work given the cruising speed he showed at Chester.
runners.comment: Ran well from the front in the Chester Cup; this 1m6f trip might be a better fit

runners.name: Yashin
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 7yo; won a slowly run, four-runner Group 3 over 2m here in April 2025 on his stable debut but failed to kick on and he needed to run a lot better than he did at Newmarket in early May (1m6f) to be seriously considered here; the hood and blinkers are new additions.
runners.comment: Has some back-class but he was worryingly poor on his reappearance

runners.name: Real Dream
runners.spotlight: Close up in a Saudi Arabian 1m7f handicap in February (latest run in a tongue-tie that now returns) but that form is none too persuasive in a race of this nature and he's hardly hit the ground running back in Britain; only eighth in this last year off 2lb higher; one for the optimists.
runners.comment: Has hardly hit the ground running since returned to Britain; one for the optimists

races.race_name: Racing To School Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Louis Veron
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on first two hurdling starts and did not need to improve to win easily at Warwick (2m, good) a fortnight ago; bred to be suited by today's longer trip and has obvious claims.
runners.comment: Did not need to improve to win easily at Warwick this month; can follow up

runners.name: Maskarvel
runners.spotlight: Had to settle for third (just behind Take The Boat) when odds-on over C&D (good to firm) 17 days ago, but showed fair form when runner-up at at Huntingdon (2m, good to soft) and Aintree (2m1f, good) this spring and has another big role to play.
runners.comment: Only third when odds-on over C&D last month but fair form when runner-up twice beforehand

runners.name: Take The Boat
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer at up to 1m6f with 4-22 strike-rate; took pretty well to hurdling and belied 20-1 odds when second (just ahead of Maskarvel) over C&D (good to firm) last month and has each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Fair Flat-racer; second over C&D on recent hurdles debut; each-way claims at least

runners.name: Angel Of My Dreams
runners.spotlight: Easily won a point bumper in March 2025 (good to soft) but did not show much in three rules bumpers (remote when second here last month); significant improvement required on hurdles debut; tongue-tie removed.
runners.comment: Won point bumper last year but did not show much in three rules bumpers; hurdles debut

runners.name: Usyk
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in run-of-the-mill bumpers around this time last year but lacked fluency and finished over 20l behind Take The Boat and Maskarvel over C&D (good to firm) on recent hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Lacked fluency and was over 20l behind two of these over C&D on recent hurdles debut

runners.name: Black Dakota
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner; not beaten far in Wexford hunter chase (2m7f, good to yielding; first-time cheekpieces) on last month's rules debut but was remote when fourth in Listowel maiden hurdle (2m4f, good to yielding) three weeks later; recently changed hands for 5,000gns; goes without headgear on this stable debut; best watched
runners.comment: Irish point winner; not beaten far in recent hunter chase but best watched on stable debut

runners.name: Tequila Star
runners.spotlight: 0-10 on the Flat and absent since running poorly in low-grade AW handicap in December; makes very limited appeal on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: 0-10 on the Flat and absent since running poorly in low-grade AW handicap in December

runners.name: Colibri Bleu
runners.spotlight: Won a two-runner maiden point in March 2025 but failed to complete his next five points, and was about 24l behind easy winner Louis Veron when third of seven on recent rules debut at Warwick (2m, good).
runners.comment: About 24l behind easy winner Louis Veron on recent rules debut at Warwick (2m)

runners.name: McGrath From Clune
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner, latterly off lowly handicap mark in the autumn, but well beaten on both starts this spring (hurdle/chase) and faces tough task on these terms; hood removed from headgear collection.
runners.comment: Well beaten both starts this spring (hurdle/chase) and faces a tough task on these terms

runners.name: Penzance
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat racer, albeit on the downgrade now; back with John Hanlon after three-run spell with Mark Loughnane that included a second on Polytrack (1m4f; RPR 88) last month; made underwhelming hurdle debut when well-held third at Worcester (2m, good) last summer but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Well held when third on last summer's hurdles debut but is still a useful Flat racer

races.race_name: Stratford Supporting Racing Staff Week Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Buxted Reel
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner; returned from break with disappointing run four weeks ago but was close up in two handicap hurdles (both 2m, good) in this month last year and has good claims if back on song.
runners.comment: Back from break with quiet run in May but on workable mark if judged on last June's form

runners.name: Northern Rose
runners.spotlight: 0-23 over hurdles but runner-up three times at this track, most recently over 2m2f (good) on latest outing in October; ran well fresh last spring and features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: 0-23 over hurdles and off since October but runs this track well and can go well fresh

runners.name: Kotgar
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles; ended 2025 with two poor efforts and was safely held in March, after a break; needs good boost from first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-9 over hurdles; safely held in March, after a break; new cheekpieces need to help

runners.name: Dublinofficecallin
runners.spotlight: Displayed improvement to make winning handicap debut for Ewan Whillans at Perth (2m4f, good) in April; reportedly unsuited by good to soft ground when well held over that C&D last month; subsequently changed hands for £15,000; not sure to be suited by drop back to 2m on this stable debut.
runners.comment: Won over 2m4f on handicap debut in Perth; may find 2m too sharp on first run for new yard

runners.name: Mayday Games
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who had a poor record for Clare Ellam last year (four runs); makes debut for latest new stable (same owner) after 254-day absence; big leap of faith required to support him.
runners.comment: Had poor record in 2025; makes stable debut after 254-day absence

runners.name: Estate Planning
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on both starts in cheekpieces (now removed) during the winter and now 0-13 over hurdles; returns from break on tempting mark and will be suited by this return to good ground but has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on both starts in cheekpieces during the winter and now 0-13 over hurdles

runners.name: Giddyupadingdong
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since winning bumper debut for another stable in 2023 and made low-key handicap debut when last of five finishers at Fontwell (2m1f, good; first-time tongue-tie) last month; may yet do better, though, and is well worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Last of five finishers on last month's handicap debut but may yet do better

runners.name: Hopjes
runners.spotlight: Lowly rated Flat maiden who has not shown any significant promise over hurdles after four attempts; has more realistic chance to get competitive on this handicap debut but still makes only limited appeal; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Lowly rated Flat maiden who has not shown any significant promise over hurdles

runners.name: Pink Walls
runners.spotlight: Struggled to get competitive off basement Flat marks in 2023 and, following a long absence, has made an inauspicious start to her hurdling career for new stable this spring; sports refitted hood for handicap hurdle debut; 7lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Poor Flat maiden who has made an inauspicious start to his hurdling career

runners.name: Papa Oscar
runners.spotlight: Down the field on both Flat starts for this stable last summer and showed no more than tiny glimmers of promise when a big-priced outsider for three maiden/novice hurdles this year; has lowly mark for this handicap debut but still needs to improve.
runners.comment: No more than tiny glimmers of promise when big outsider for three maiden/novice hurdles

runners.name: Hellion
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on all three hurdling starts for new stable this year but displayed clear promise when third on both Flat runs last summer and may yet transfer some of that ability to this discipline; sports first-time cheekpieces for this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well beaten on all three hurdling starts this year but showed clear promise on the Flat

runners.name: Cosmic Spirit
runners.spotlight: Well beaten when 66-1 for handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m, heavy) in February but remains unexposed and could plausibly be seen in a much better light on this forecast quicker surface.
runners.comment: Well beaten on handicap debut in February but the ground was heavy; improvement possible

runners.name: Lheur De Gloire
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who came from off the pace to win on last month's chasing debut at Market Rasen (2m1f, good; refitted tongue-tie); 6lb higher in a stronger race but should have more to offer.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 5yo who come from off the pace to won on chase debut at Market Rasen in May

runners.name: In The Air
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over fences (once over C&D) in early part of last season; returned from break with two below-par hurdle runs this spring but could be back at concert pitch for this return to chasing; a possible.
runners.comment: Needs to better recent hurdling form but won three chases (one over C&D) last year

runners.name: Whatyouwaitingfor
runners.spotlight: 0-3 over fences but ran well when clear third over C&D (good to firm) last month, despite making two significant jumping errors in the closing stages; competes off same mark today; one to consider.
runners.comment: 0-3 over fences but ran well when third over C&D last month, despite late jumping errors

runners.name: Nibras Gold
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in three of his four chases, most recently at Bangor (2m1f, good; well clear of third) last month, after a layoff; also suited by this track and can make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Clear second at Bangor last month, after a break, and also suited by this track

runners.name: Seeyouinmydreams
runners.spotlight: Free-going front-runner; signed off for Paul Nicholls with wide-margin success in modest selling hurdle last month and remains on a workable handicap mark but capitulated pretty tamely when well-held third of four in Plumpton chase (2m1f, good) for new stable three weeks ago; comes with risk.
runners.comment: Free-going front-runner; won selling hurdle last month; well held in a chase for new yard

runners.name: Boom Boom
runners.spotlight: Won twice here last summer (2m1f/2m3f) but went right off the boil in the autumn and was pulled up when reappearing here (2m5f) last month; 11lb out of the weights today; has too much to prove.
runners.comment: Won twice here last summer but has lost his way since and is 11lb wrong today

runners.name: Not So Sobers
runners.spotlight: Made all over hurdles at Hereford (2m, good) last month and followed up on the Flat 17 days ago; not bred for fences but evidently in good nick; no surprise if he makes a bold bid on chasing debut.
runners.comment: Not bred for fences but won over hurdles and on the Flat last month; interesting

runners.name: Edelak
runners.spotlight: 1m4f Flat winner in Ireland last summer (useful form); not an instant success over hurdles but justified skinny odds with very comfortable win in run-of-the-mill Huntingdon maiden (2m, good to firm) last month and could have a lot more to offer in handicaps.
runners.comment: Won maiden hurdle very comfortably last month; should have more to offer in handicaps

runners.name: Machete Beach
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat in 2024 and a dual hurdle winner last month, latterly here (2m2f, good to firm); ought to make a bold bid from the front on this handicap hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Useful on the Flat in 2024 and a dual hurdle winner last month, latterly here (2m2f)

runners.name: Hooper
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 24l when fifth of seven on last month's stable debut at Ludlow (2m, good) but that was his first run for 498 days and some positives can be drawn from it; worth a second look on this return to Stratford, given he was very well suited by this track when a useful performer for Nicky Henderson a few years ago; tongue-tie fitted for first time.
runners.comment: Safely held on stable debut but has some useful C&D form on his CV; dangerous to ignore

races.race_name: Happy 80th Birthday David Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Two To Tango
runners.spotlight: In the frame all six starts since switch to chasing in the autumn, including course win (2m3f, good) in first-time cheekpieces in March; ran big race when second (albeit well held in the end) over 2m5f here last month; strong claims if stamina holds out over this longer trip.
runners.comment: In the frame all six chase starts, including course win in March; respected

runners.name: Harrys Hope
runners.spotlight: 5-10 over fences, including good-ground C&D win; absent since safely held in competitive race at Cheltenham in October but resumes on a workable mark and has won when fresh before; respected.
runners.comment: 5-10 over fences (includes C&D wins); off since October but has won when fresh before

runners.name: Grillon De Monty
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner at Worcester (2m7f, good) last summer; gave good account when second over C&D (good to firm; first-time hood) last month, after a break, but the market principals in that race disappointed and the form is of questionable value; others look better handicapped.
runners.comment: Won twice last summer; ran well here last month but the form is slightly dubious

runners.name: Tamarind Bay
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles but returned from break with two pretty good efforts towards end of last season, latterly third off today's mark at Newcastle (2m6f, good to soft); has a fighting chance if he takes well to chasing.
runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles but ended last season in good form; has a fighting chance on chase debut

runners.name: Mix Of Clover
runners.spotlight: Long in the tooth and rather inconsistent nowadays but only 2lb higher than for course win (2m5f, good) in October and might have needed the run when well-held third (behind Two To Tango) over that trip here last month; could have a say.
runners.comment: Long in the tooth and inconsistent nowadays but won here in the October; not ruled out

races.race_name: 19th July Is Ladies Day Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Raffles Nobu
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his French hurdle win in 2023 and didn't find much for pressure when fourth off this much-reduced mark at Worcester (2m, good) on Thursday; that certainly wasn't a bad run but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ran okay when fourth off this much-reduced mark on Thursday but remains winless since 2023

runners.name: Ocean Heights
runners.spotlight: Ended winter AW campaign with respectable fourth in February and kept on for third when back over hurdles at Newton Abbot (2m2f, good) three weeks ago; also suited by this track and can go well again; swaps cheekpieces for refitted visor.
runners.comment: Kept on for third at Newton Abbot when back over hurdles last month; can go well again

runners.name: Saint Polo
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on last month's chasing debut (first-time cheekpieces) but ran well on consecutive hurdling starts beforehand, more notably when clear second at Sedgefield (2m4f, good) on first occasion; has tongue-tie added for this return to hurdling; dangerous if quickly back in the groove.
runners.comment: Well beaten on last month's chasing debut but earlier hurdling form gives him a good shout

runners.name: Backer Bilk
runners.spotlight: Won a low-grade AW handicap last year and has shown some aptitude for hurdling this spring, making the frame in run-of-the-mill 2m maidens at Hereford (good) and Huntingdon (good to firm); not obviously well treated for this handicap debut and not bred for the longer trip, though.
runners.comment: Flat winner; has shown aptitude for hurdling this spring; makes handicap debut

runners.name: Hiconic
runners.spotlight: Seven-time hurdle winner (once over C&D); runner-up on the AW in January and returned to this discipline with very respectable fourth at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last month; likely contender.
runners.comment: C&D winner; returned to hurdling with pretty good run at Fontwell last month; good chance

races.race_name: British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Talitha Rouge
runners.spotlight: 20-1, made a promising start with a clear third of six in maiden at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago; open to progress and a likely player.
runners.comment: Promising start with clear third at Redcar; she's capable of better; a likely player

runners.name: Hello Friend
runners.spotlight: 13-2, encouraging debut second of six in 1m4f novice on Newcastle AW four weeks ago; this Frankel filly must enter calculations with better to come.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut second at Newcastle; must enter calculations with progress on the cards

runners.name: Hapiness
runners.spotlight: 3-1, shaped well when a neck second of six in maiden at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) on her debut 26 days ago; pulled clear of the rest there and she holds leading claims with improvement very much on the cards.
runners.comment: Shaped well when neck second at Chepstow on debut; leading claims with more to come

runners.name: Counter Intuitive
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly who took a big step forward from her debut eighth when running on into sixth of 13 in 1m2f Salisbury novice last month; she can do better still, especially over this longer trip, so needs considering.
runners.comment: Built on debut with sixth at Salisbury latest; can do better still up in trip; considered

runners.name: Galistra
runners.spotlight: Galiway filly who beat just one in both AW starts in 1m3f Kempton maiden and 1m4f Southwell novice early in 2026 for Ralph Beckett; since changed hands for 6,000gns and returns after wind surgery for her new yard with plenty more required.
runners.comment: Well held both starts for Ralph Beckett; back after wind op for new yard with more needed

runners.name: Ice Cold Alex
runners.spotlight: A fair 5f juvenile winner; back to form with fourth in 5f Beverley handicap in May and didn't enjoy a smooth run (also stumbled 1f out) when sixth of seven at Pontefract (5f) last time; possibilities.
runners.comment: Didn't enjoy smooth run when sixth of seven at Pontefract (5f) last time; possibilities

runners.name: Golden Havana
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with victory on Newcastle AW (5f) in December and he's continued in good form, third of six in another 5f handicap there two weeks ago; firmly in the picture back on turf eased 1lb.
runners.comment: In good form, third at Newcastle two weeks ago; firmly in the picture back on turf

runners.name: Dragon Spin
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win at the eighth attempt in 5f Nottingham handicap in May but she failed to back it up when sixth of 11 over C&D 11 days ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: First win at Nottingham but just sixth over C&D 11 days ago; needs to get back on track

runners.name: U Turn
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win in 5f Beverley maiden last July and ended 2025 with good placed efforts in 5f nurseries over C&D and at Musselburgh in September; resumes from nine months off, having been gelded, but not out of things for his in-form yard.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with good placed efforts over C&D and Musselburgh; not out of things on return

runners.name: Riffa Spirit
runners.spotlight: Landed 6f Chelmsford AW novice for Simon and Ed Crisford as 2yo; shaped encouragingly for new stable when in the frame on Southwell AW and at Musselburgh but raced too freely when last of eight at Redcar (5f) last time; blinkers are reached for (tongue-tie also refitted); could bounce back.
runners.comment: Too free when last at Redcar latest; blinkers are reached for now and could bounce back

runners.name: Simba's Pride
runners.spotlight: Looked on the up when bagging 6f handicaps at Hamilton and Ripon in May; not discredited (did best of those in near-side group) when fifth of 13 at York (5f, good) four days ago; player.
runners.comment: Scored at Hamilton and Ripon; not discredited when fifth at York four days ago; player

runners.name: Arlo's Girl
runners.spotlight: Landed 5f AW Lingfield novice on her debut in August but she's failed to go on in three subsequent runs, racing too freely when beating just one in 5f Goodwood handicap 24 days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Won 5f AW Lingfield novice on debut in August but failed to go on in three runs since

runners.name: Bravo Hotel
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of his five starts as 2yo but he signed off with a well-held third of four on his handicap debut at Catterick (5f) in October; needs to take a step forward on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with well-held third of four at Catterick in October; needs to step forward

runners.name: Pull The Rug
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f winner as 2yo; nearest at the finish when a good fourth of 11 in 5f handicap here 11 days ago; very much one to consider back up in trip with a visor added.
runners.comment: Good fourth here 11 days ago; very much one to consider back up in trip with a visor added

runners.name: Frantic
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly who took a step forward on her handicap debut when second (for the third time) of eight at Leicester (6f) in May, coming home clear of her group; up 2lb but has more to offer and can gain a deserved first win.
runners.comment: Step forward on h'cap debut with Leicester second latest (first home in group); big player

runners.name: Space Bear
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win for her current yard at Yarmouth (6f) in April but she failed to beat a rival when tailed off in nine-runner handicap at Ripon (6f) 30 days ago when back on turf; has something to prove now.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival when tailed off at Ripon (6f) 30 days ago; something to prove now

runners.name: Taygar
runners.spotlight: Scored at Ayr (6f) last July and she arrives in very good nick, beaten just a head over C&D 11 days ago having hung left late on; must enter calculations nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Beaten a head over C&D 11 days ago having hung left late; must enter calculations up 1lb

runners.name: Sugar Baby
runners.spotlight: Scored four times (5f/6f) in 2025; shaped encouragingly when seventh of 11 in 6f Redcar handicap 22 days ago, not enjoying the smoothest of passages; considered off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Encouraging seventh at Redcar 22 days ago; not out of things off a 2lb lower mark
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Kings Merchant
runners.spotlight: On a losing run and he beat only one in 7f Carlisle handicap 18 days ago; this C&D winner needs to take a big step forward in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: On a losing run and he beat only one in 7f Carlisle handicap 18 days ago; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Harlequin Bay
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up in maidens (5f/6f) on Newcastle AW late in 2025; beat only one after four months off on his turf/handicap debut at Haydock 26 days ago, however.
runners.comment: Beat only one after four months off on turf/handicap debut at Haydock 26 days ago

runners.name: Record Day
runners.spotlight: Brought along steadily but he failed to improve on handicap debut when in midfield at York (7f) 24 days ago; he may still do better here though.
runners.comment: Failed to improve on handicap debut when in midfield at York; may still do better here

runners.name: Tactical Formation
runners.spotlight: In the frame on all his three runs as 2yo; visored and failed to improve on his handicap debut when fifth of eight on Southwell AW (7f) in January; cheekpieces fitted with more required after a break.
runners.comment: Failed to improve on h'cap debut with Southwell AW fifth; cheekpieces on now after a break

runners.name: Emperor Caradoc
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Thirsk (6f) in May but comes here on the back of a below-par fifth of nine in 6f Pontefract handicap two weeks ago; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Won at Thirsk in May but was a below-par fifth at Pontefract two weeks ago; more needed

runners.name: Vingegaard
