runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 5f winner in 2024 for Archie Watson; missed 2025 and yet to show that form for his current yard this season, finishing last of six in 5f Hamilton handicap 20 days ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Last of six in 5f Hamilton handicap 20 days ago; others are preferred

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Zarvali
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden at 2yrs for Johnny Murtagh; improved to make a winning start for Roger Fell in 7f Catterick novice in May and he shaped promisingly when sixth of 20 in 7f York handicap 24 days ago, finishing well having not enjoyed a clear run; major player eased 1lb
runners.comment: Promising sixth of 20 at York latest, finishing well having not enjoyed clear run; player

runners.name: Inside Story
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f juvenile maiden winner; has made a positive start to life in handicaps, a free-going fourth of nine at Lingfield (7f) last month; she may do better, especially if settling better.
runners.comment: Free-going fourth at Lingfield last month; may do better, especially if settling better

runners.name: Woodstock
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 but he comes here in good form, fourth of 11 to This Farh in 7.5f Beverley handicap 20 days ago; considered.
runners.comment: Winless since 2024 but comes here in good form, fourth at Beverley 20 days ago; considered

runners.name: City Captain
runners.spotlight: Scored four times in 2025 and has started this season in very good form, runner-up over C&D before fourth of ten at Haydock (7f) last month when doing best of the pacesetters; very much one to consider.
runners.comment: In very good form, fourth at Haydock latest when doing best of pacesetters; likely player

runners.name: Kosometsuke
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start for Michael Dods over C&D in April but he failed to beat a rival in 7f Mussleburgh handicap 26 days ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: C&D winner; failed to beat a rival in 7f Mussleburgh handicap 26 days ago; more needed

runners.name: It Just Takes Time
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who bounced back to his best with second of 14 in 7f Wetherby handicap 12 days ago; up 2lb but he remains 1lb below his last winning mark so merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: C&D winner; back on song with Wetherby second 12 days ago; merits serious consideration

runners.name: This Farh
runners.spotlight: Made all in handicaps at Leicester (7f) and Beverley (7.5f) this spring and posted another good effort with fourth of ten in 7f Chepstow handicap ten days ago; ought to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Another good effort with fourth in Chepstow handicap ten days ago; ought to be thereabouts

runners.name: Fuji Mountain
runners.spotlight: Without a victory since 2024 and he arrives well below par; reared and unseated rider at the start in 6f Wolverhampton handicap 15 days ago.
runners.comment: Arrives well below par; reared and unseated rider start at Wolverhampton 15 days ago

runners.name: Mr King
runners.spotlight: Winless for Iain Jardine (also refused to race once); failed to build on Redcar sixth for his new yard when seventh of ten in 1m Carlisle handicap 17 days ago; not one to rely on.
runners.comment: Failed to build on Redcar sixth for new yard when seventh at Carlisle; not one to rely on

runners.name: Gressington
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 7f win at Newcastle but he's been below par this season, too free when eighth of 14 in 6f Carlisle handicap 17 days ago; more is required despite a falling mark.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with 7f win at Newcastle but he's been below par this season
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Ormolulu
runners.spotlight: Quirky sort but she scored twice last term and wasn't seen to ideal effect from a wide draw when tenth of 22 in 7f York handicap last month; not out of things off a 2lb lower mark, especially if starting better.
runners.comment: Quirky sort but not best drawn when tenth at York latest; not out of things off 2lb lower

runners.name: Degale
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who bounced back to form when third at Nottingham (1m) in May; had too much use made of him when last of 11 in Ayr handicap (1m) since and not written off.
runners.comment: Too much use made of him when last of 11 in Ayr handicap last time and not written off

runners.name: Marajito
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 under a cloud but right back to her best with victory in 5f Ripon handicap 19 days ago; up 5lb but that form has been franked so she's a likely player.
runners.comment: Back to her best with Ripon win 19 days ago; up 5lb but form franked so a likely player

runners.name: Spendmore Lane
runners.spotlight: Notched a third AW victory of 2026 when prevailing at Lingfield (5f) in April and she posted another good effort when third of ten in 5f Wolverhampton handicap 18 days ago; ought to be in the shake-up once more.
runners.comment: Good third at Wolverhampton 18 days ago; ought to be in the shake-up once more

runners.name: Military Girl
runners.spotlight: Better than ever this season and she went 2-3 for the campaign in 11-runner handicap over C&D 11 days ago; showed a good attitude there so merits serious consideration nudged up 2lb.
runners.comment: Went 2-3 this term with C&D win 11 days ago; merits serious consideration nudged up 2lb

runners.name: Emerald Harmony
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but she comes here in decent nick for her current yard, third of seven at Nottingham (5f) 16 days ago; needs considering on a 2lb lower mark here.
runners.comment: On a losing run but she comes here in decent nick; needs considering on a 2lb lower mark

runners.name: Pal Joey
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with 5f success at Newcastle in January but he's failed to reproduce it, again slowly away when fifth of seven at Hamilton (5f) six days ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Scored at Newcastle in January but he's failed to reproduce it; others are preferred

runners.name: Khabib
runners.spotlight: Fair and consistent for Ray Craggs in 2024; off 20 months before coming in last of nine, on sole outing for David Thompson, over 6f here last month; has rejoined his former yard with lots to find.
runners.comment: Off 20 months before last of nine for David Thompson here; has rejoined his former yard

runners.name: Beaumadier
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Wolverhampton (5f) in February but below form since, too free when fourth of six there (5f) following month; needs to bounce back returned to turf.
runners.comment: Resumed winning ways at Wolverhampton (5f) in February but below form since
runners.owner: R M Brilley, A E Townsend & Partner

runners.name: Hover On The Wind
runners.spotlight: Signed off for 2025 with 5f win at Newcastle in December and largely in good form this season, fourth of ten in 5f Southwell handicap 26 days ago; not out of things eased 2lb.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with 5f win at Newcastle in December and largely in good form this season

runners.name: King's Crown
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who got back on track with third of ten in 5f Pontefract handicap 25 days ago; in the mix again.
runners.comment: C&D winner who got back on track with third in Pontefract handicap 25 days ago; in the mix

runners.name: Le Beau Garcon
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer but he failed to build on Ripon reappearance fourth when only fifth of seven in 5f Catterick handicap five days ago; he's not the easiest to catch right.
runners.comment: C&D scorer but only fifth of seven at Catterick five days ago; not easiest to catch right

races.race_name: Next Meeting Wednesday 1st July - Book Now Handicap

runners.name: Crocus Time
runners.spotlight: Thriving for her current yard and she arrives on a hat-trick after landing handicaps at Redcar (5f) and Wetherby (5.5f); up another 4lb but this course scorer remains handily weighted on old form; player.
runners.comment: On hat-trick after scoring at Redcar and Wetherby; this course winner rates a big player

runners.name: Irish Dancer
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Catterick (5f) in May but he was well below par when eighth of 12 over C&D 11 days ago; this C&D scorer is the sort to bounce back though; blinkers refitted.
runners.comment: Won at Catterick; well below par over C&D latest; this C&D scorer is sort to bounce back

runners.name: Blue Lakota
runners.spotlight: In very good form on the AW through the winter (scored twice at Newcastle) but he's yet to show any worthwhile form on turf and came in last of ten in 5f Carlisle handicap last time; others appeal much more.
runners.comment: In very good form on the AW this winter but yet to show any worthwhile form on turf

runners.name: Startling
runners.spotlight: A fair 5f scorer for David O'Meara in 2025; posted a good third of 11 for her new stable over C&D 11 days ago so must enter calculations off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Good third for her new stable over C&D 11 days ago; must enter calculations off same mark

runners.name: Knicks
runners.spotlight: Winless since May 2024 but he took a step back in the right direction with a close third of seven in 5f Pontefract handicap eight days ago; very much one to consider off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction with Pontefract third latest; very much one to consider

runners.name: Midnight Lir
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence but he got back on track in first-time blinkers (tongue-tie also back on) with third of ten in 5f Carlisle handicap 18 days ago; well in the mix off the same mark with headgear retained.
runners.comment: Back on track in first-time blinkers with third at Carlisle latest; well in the mix

runners.name: Westgate Warrior
runners.spotlight: A fair 5f scorer in 2025; back to form with runner-up at Nottingham (5f) in May but he hung left when beating just two at Southwell (5f) last time; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Hung left when beating just two at Southwell (5f) last time; needs to get back on track

runners.name: Invincible Crown
runners.spotlight: A dual 5f scorer at Wolverhampton in 2025; yet to hit top form this season, reared at start when seventh of 12 over C&D 11 days ago.
runners.comment: A dual 5f scorer at Wolverhampton in 2025; yet to hit top form this season though
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing VIII & Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Dc Cogent
runners.spotlight: On a very long losing run and he came in last of eight in 5f Ripon handicap 19 days ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: On a very long losing run and he came in last of eight in 5f Ripon handicap 19 days ago
runners.owner: J R Dwyer And B Smart

runners.name: Dandy Dinmont
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Beverley (5f) in May but failed to beat a rival in ten-runner handicap at Carlisle (5f) 18 days ago; this course scorer has a bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival at Carlisle (5f) 18 days ago; this course scorer has a bit to prove

runners.name: Albegone
runners.spotlight: Arrives in good nick without winning this season, third of six in 5f Nottingham handicap five days ago; ought to be in the shake-up again.
runners.comment: Arrives in good nick without winning this season; ought to be in the shake-up again
runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Pentonville
runners.spotlight: Made a long odds-on favourite work for it, despite having raced freely in a small field, on his handicap debut (1m2f; first-time cheekpieces) three weeks ago; this 295,000gns yearling remains open to improvement and is high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Solid handicap debut (1m2f; first-time cheekpieces); open to improvement; shortlisted
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Imola
runners.spotlight: Plenty of short-priced defeats to her name but goes well here and is chasing a course hat-trick after two wins over 9.5f, the latest a fortnight ago following a break (Silky Lass back in third); decent chance upped 4lb.
runners.comment: Chasing a course hat-trick after two wins over 9.5f; decent chance upped 4lb

runners.name: Volto Di Medusa
runners.spotlight: Hasn't always looked straightforward but found things falling his way when winning narrowly off 2lb lower on Polytrack two weeks ago; fine on Tapeta but his jockey, who doesn't claim, has only ridden twice under rules, including once over hurdles in Ireland.
runners.comment: Won narrowly off 2lb lower on Polytrack two weeks ago; fine on Tapeta; each-way claims

runners.name: Silky Lass
runners.spotlight: Did well to get as close as she did over C&D last month and backed that up when third to Imola here latest; 4lb better off tonight, plus has Jack Lander, who's ridden two winners for the yard, taking off 5lb; player.
runners.comment: Did well to get so close over C&D last month; backed up when third to Imola latest; player

runners.name: Hashtagnotions
runners.spotlight: Dual Newcastle winner who added two turf victories last autumn, all off lower marks; consistently below market expectations of late and will want to settle better than over C&D last time, coming from stall 13 under a jockey taking just her third ride.
runners.comment: Consistently below market expectations of late; will want to settle better from stall 13

runners.name: Quick Quasar
runners.spotlight: Inexperience cost him on his handicap debut (Tapeta; first-time cheekpieces), having been gelded, and he made no mistake at Kempton next time; 9lb rise to overcome as he goes beyond 7f for the first time but he's unexposed still.
runners.comment: 9lb rise to overcome as he goes beyond 7f for the first time but he's unexposed still

runners.name: Ebn Sabt
runners.spotlight: Poor debut for his new trainer on soft ground ten days ago; went close here for his previous yard last autumn but has never been quite this far and is hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Poor debut for his new trainer on soft ground ten days ago; hard to recommend

runners.name: This Rib
runners.spotlight: Easy to back ahead of his handicap debut last month, having been gelded, when bumped early and pulled up (said to have stopped quickly); Kayleigh McCammon takes just her fifth ride under rules.
runners.comment: Easy to back ahead of his handicap debut last month, when pulled up; has it to prove

runners.name: Rhythm N Rock
runners.spotlight: He's done most of his racing at Kempton and hasn't shone in two starts round here; popped up off a 4lb lower mark last time, back from a short break, but will need to better that under Ruby Criscuolo, who's taking just her fifth ride under rules.
runners.comment: Done most of his racing at Kempton; needs more upped 4lb under an inexperienced partner

runners.name: Sandret
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Rebecca Hitchen last summer; fine on Tapeta but returns from ten months off looking high enough in the weights and he's entitled to need the run at his age.
runners.comment: Two wins for Rebecca Hitchen last summer; entitled to need the run at his age

runners.name: Molly Valentine
runners.spotlight: String of poor efforts since going close for Dean Ivory here last June; would be a surprise winner back in headgear under Jake Holman (0-7 to date).
runners.comment: String of poor efforts since going close for Dean Ivory here last June; be a shock winner

runners.name: Pipily
runners.spotlight: £50,000 breeze-up 2yo who finished well held in three turf runs last summer (6f-7f); the market will be the ultimate guide now handicapping in first-time headgear back from almost a year off.
runners.comment: Market the ultimate guide now handicapping in first-time headgear after almost a year off

runners.name: Harbour Vision
runners.spotlight: Three wins here in the winter to take his course tally to 11 but he's rather lost the plot since and doesn't often go beyond 7f these days; needs a revival.
runners.comment: He's rather lost the plot and doesn't often go beyond 7f these days; needs a revival

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com EBF Restricted Novice (EBF Restriced Race Qual') (GBB) (Div I)

runners.name: Ballisty
runners.spotlight: Third of six at Goodwood (5f, good) and front-running second of 13 at Newmarket (6f, good) two weeks later, both suggest a leading role today.
runners.comment: Goodwood 3rd (5f, good) and front-running Newmarket 2nd (6f, good) suggest a leading role

runners.name: Duidin
runners.spotlight: 9-2 when third of ten at Kempton (6f, AW) in April, racing wide; eighth of 13 at Newmarket (6f, good) was modest in comparison but he was 14-1 in that and today's race gives him realistic claims if he returns to debut form.
runners.comment: Modest on turf last time but he has realistic claims if he returns to debut AW form

runners.name: Ventura Power
runners.spotlight: Modest form recently at Windsor (6f/5f, good to firm) on both starts, which gives him a place chance but extra is needed for the win.
runners.comment: Modest form recently at Windsor (6f/5f, good to firm) both starts; needs extra for the win

runners.name: Laylaty
runners.spotlight: Second foal; Ardad half-sister to 7f-1m2f winner Big Song (including 2yo/Qatar; RPR 94); dam 1m4f winner (83) out of unraced half-sister to the Canadian 1m4f Grade 1 winner and 2m4f Gold Cup third Sheikhzayedroad; early days for the yard's 2yos and this one looks bred for further.
runners.comment: Ardad half-sister to 7f-1m2f winner Big Song (including 2yo; RPR 94); dam 1m4f winner (83)

runners.name: Ouragan
runners.spotlight: 10,000euros yearling, £15,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; Bayside Boy half-sister to 6f/7f winner Ghost Mode (including 2yo/AW; RPR 98); dam unraced sister to 6f AW winner Elisheva, closely related to AW winners Bosstime (1m) and Refusetolisten (7f); stable's 2yos have made a splash this term.
runners.comment: £15,000 breeze-up 2yo by Bayside Boy; stable's 2yos have made a splash this term

runners.name: Ten Of Clubs
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling by Showcasing; second foal; dam twice-raced half-sister to winners including Taamol (7f/1m Listed), Botas (6.5f-1m1f) and Queen Of Seduction (11.6f/1m4f); trainer had a close second with 2yo newcomer (12-1) ten days ago.
runners.comment: 22,000gns yearling by Showcasing; yard had close 2nd with 2yo newcomer (12-1) ten days ago

runners.name: Lopefernza
runners.spotlight: By Lope Y Fernandez; fifth foal; dam 9.4f-1m4f AW winner (RPR 74), half-sister to 6f-1m winner Limit Up, is granddaughter of 1m3f-winning sister to Oaks winner Love Divine; trainer had two 2yo winners last week, who had already had one and two runs respectively.
runners.comment: By Lope Y Fernandez; dam 9.4f-1m4f winner (RPR 74); trainer had two 2yo winners last week

runners.name: Alpha Legend
runners.spotlight: Stamina in his pedigree; Leicester eighth on debut was better than Doncaster eighth (again 5f on good to firm) three weeks later, and neither puts him into serious contention in this field; tongue-tie goes on.
runners.comment: Stamina in pedigree; neither run puts him into serious contention; tongue-tie goes on
runners.owner: Heart Of The South Racing 146 & Partner

runners.name: Lincoln Warrior
runners.spotlight: Bred for further; went down by 11l when sixth of seven in novice at Carlisle (5f, good; 12-1) 18 days ago, making most; no form claims yet.
runners.comment: 11l sixth of seven in novice at Carlisle (5f, good; 12-1) 18 days ago, making most
runners.owner: Spg Property, Alfa Site & Graeme Curnow

runners.name: South West
runners.spotlight: 5-1 for maiden at Bath (5.5f, good) 11 days ago but no show after blowing the start; hopefully all the wiser this time.
runners.comment: Hopefully all the wiser after blowing the start at Bath (5.5f, good; 5-1) 11 days ago

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com EBF Restricted Novice (EBF Restriced Race Qual') (GBB) (Div II)

runners.name: Hadlan
runners.spotlight: 38,000gns breeze-up 2yo; 40-1, never-dangerous, 7l seventh of 12 in novice at Nottingham (6f, good) 13 days ago; this field brings very little form but he has the best of it, albeit modest.
runners.comment: 38,000gns breeze-up 2yo; 40-1, never-dangerous, 7l seventh of 12 at Nottingham (6f, good)

runners.name: King Koopa
runners.spotlight: 12,000euros foal, 40,000euros yearling by Cotai Glory; second foal; closely related to 8.6f 2yo/9.4f AW winner Zipwire (RPR 71); dam unraced half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Aussie Reigns, out of unraced sister to 1m Listed winner Zayn Zen; yard 0-17 with 2yos this term before Monday.
runners.comment: 40,000euros yearling by Cotai Glory; yard 0-17 with 2yos this term before Monday

runners.name: Macau
runners.spotlight: 16,000gns foal, 40,000euros yearling by Cotai Glory; second foal; half-brother to 7f/7.3f 2yo winner Astrazar (RPR 86); dam placed at 7f on AW as 2yo (82), half-sister to winners Swift Approval (German 7f Listed) and Aurora Eclipse (5f 2yo/6f); trainer's record means he needs a close look.
runners.comment: 40,000euros yearling by Cotai Glory; trainer's record means he needs a close look

runners.name: Angel Ang
runners.spotlight: 8,000euros yearling; eighth foal; Supremacy half-brother to five winners including Prefontaine (1m2f-1m4f including 2yo/KSA; RPR 92) and Multicultural (7f; 84); dam 6f AW 2yo/1m turf winner (100); trainer 4-24 with this season's 2yos before Monday.
runners.comment: 8,000euros yearling by Supremacy; trainer 4-24 with this season's 2yos before Monday

runners.name: Patronise
runners.spotlight: 16-1, raced wide when always behind at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month; interesting only if strong in the market, which may be possible in this weak race.
runners.comment: 16-1, raced wide when always behind at Windsor (6f, good to firm); needs a market check

runners.name: Havana Court
runners.spotlight: 7,000gns foal, 20,000gns yearling by Havana Gold; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Signcastle City (6f-1m including 2yo), Forest Gunner (5f) and Style King (6f), out of 7f/1m-winning half-sister to Listed winners Drill (1m/1m2f) and Pinzolo (1m2f); second 2yo of the season for his yard.
runners.comment: 20,000gns yearling by Havana Gold; second 2yo of the season for his yard

runners.name: Comeonfeelthenoise
runners.spotlight: 26,000euros foal, 32,000euros yearling; by Cotai Glory; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to 5f/6f AW winner Piperstown, out of 6f AW winning sister to 5f Group 1 (Abbaye) winner Move In Time; trainer has had two 2yo winners this term, one on debut; needs considering.
runners.comment: 32,000euros yearling by Cotai Glory; trainer had two 2yo winners this term, one on debut

runners.name: Cool King
runners.spotlight: 30,000gns yearling, £25,000 breeze-up 2yo by Kingman; first foal; dam unraced sister to smart 7f/7.4f winner Bravais, out of 6f 2yo Group 2 winning half-sister to 1,000 Guineas and Oaks winner Love; trainer had a 2yo debutante (13-2) win on May 31st.
runners.comment: £25,000 breeze-up 2yo by Kingman; trainer had a 2yo debutante (13-2) win on May 31st

runners.name: Radahn
runners.spotlight: 14,000euros foal, 34,000gns yearling by Dark Angel; first foal; dam unraced sister to US 1m winner Golden Plume, half-sister to winners Bracing Breeze (7f 2yo) and Rip Roaring (1m 2yo), out of French Oaks winner; late foal; trainer's 2yos this term have done well and this one needs a market check.
runners.comment: 34,000gns yearling by Dark Angel; late foal; trainer's 2yos this term have done well

runners.name: Zohar
runners.spotlight: 25-1 when about 9l ninth of 13 at Newmarket (6f, good; 25-1) on debut, which hinted at ability, but he was beaten about twice that far at Nottingham (6f, good; 14-1) after getting messed around in the early stages.
runners.comment: Newmarket debut hinted at ability but he got messed around early on at Nottingham

runners.name: Filly Foden
runners.spotlight: Dual 5f winner early in the year who hasn't looked to see it out in a handful of runs over this trip; back on a workable mark after two lesser efforts on turf but the sixth furlong is the concern.
runners.comment: Hasn't looked to see it out in a handful of runs over this trip; back on a workable mark

runners.name: Go Lockers Go
runners.spotlight: Won his first two in the spring (Polytrack, turf), going 11lb up the weights in the process; maybe found his revised mark beyond him last time but won't mind coming back up from 5f from a handy draw; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Won his first two in the spring; won't mind coming back up from 5f from a handy draw
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing D Turner B Roberts

runners.name: King Of Chaos
runners.spotlight: Only finished placed once from nine runs in handicaps (over C&D in the winter) and is fully exposed; hasn't always looked straightforward, hanging right more than once, and remains vulnerable to improvers.
runners.comment: Hasn't always looked straightforward (can hang right) and remains vulnerable to improvers

runners.name: Beach Partee
runners.spotlight: Plenty of racing last year and this and steps back up from 5f for the first time in a while; will be doing well to make the frame.
runners.comment: Plenty of racing last year and this; steps back up from 5f for the first time in a while

runners.name: Victor Cee
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard and didn't see out 7f last time in a first-time visor (removed); handicapper quick to relent and the return to Tapeta looks a plus but he's fully exposed.
runners.comment: Handicapper quick to relent and the return to Tapeta looks a plus but he's fully exposed

runners.name: Enter Sandman
runners.spotlight: String of solid efforts in the tongue-tie/cheekpieces combination prior to a poor one last time; that came on turf, though, and he'll be happier back on the AW and over an easy 6f, plus he's well drawn for one happiest up front for a yard in fine form of late.
runners.comment: Be happier back on the AW and over an easy 6f, plus he's well drawn for his in-form yard

runners.name: Wyle Cop
runners.spotlight: Made all when winning his maiden over C&D in March (stall 11 doesn't look ideal); completely lost the plot of late, including twice in cheekpieces, and is now given a go in first-time blinkers; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Lost the plot of late and is now given a go in first-time blinkers; plenty to prove

runners.name: Nzuri
runners.spotlight: Split two subsequent winners when outrunning her odds from the front in a Kempton maiden early last month; easy to back on her handicap debut (turf) last time, having got loose beforehand; it will be interesting to see how she goes in the market this time.
runners.comment: Got loose before handicap debut; interesting to see how she goes in the market this time

runners.name: Tie Fighter
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden (5f-7f) who finished well held in first-time cheekpieces on his handicap debut early in the month; now tried in blinkers and needs to improve on his previous AW work.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden; now tried in blinkers and needs to improve on his previous AW work

runners.name: She's Divine
runners.spotlight: Solid debut over C&D this time last year but hasn't built on it, pulling hard a couple of times; absent for five months since an ordinary handicap debut at Southwell; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Solid debut over C&D this time last year but hasn't built on it; others make more appeal

runners.name: Goldenstateofmind
runners.spotlight: On the go early last season before missing the best part of a year; struggled badly in two runs back, having been gelded, and much will depend on first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Struggled badly in two runs back and much will depend on first-time headgear

runners.name: Guernsey Angel
runners.spotlight: Her two nursery wins late last year, including here, came from the front over 5f and she's rather struggled since; didn't see out this trip on soft ground last time and stall 12 can only look a hindrance.
runners.comment: Her wins came from the front over 5f; stall 12 can only look a hindrance

races.race_name: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap

runners.name: Keep It Classic
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at a short price without headgear last time and it's no surprise to see the cheekpieces back in place; holds obvious claims on his previous Haydock near-miss.
runners.comment: No surprise to see the cheekpieces back in place; good chance on penultimate effort

runners.name: Lady Lauren
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden who cracked late on her first go at 1m2f last time; stamina is an obvious concern going up in trip again on her AW debut.
runners.comment: Stamina is an obvious concern going up in trip again on her AW debut

runners.name: Risk Averse Rebel
runners.spotlight: Far from straightforward, often pulling hard and again edging left under pressure over C&D last time; that effort proved this trip within range, though, and he's clearly happier on Tapeta than turf.
runners.comment: Latest C&D effort proved this trip within range; clearly happier on Tapeta than turf

runners.name: Mick The Hat
runners.spotlight: Improved on his maiden/novice efforts when stepped up to this trip under Jack Dace on his handicap debut (0-70; turf) 12 days ago; hit a bit of traffic there and goes on the shortlist at this lower level.
runners.comment: Hit a bit of traffic when an improved third latest; on the shortlist at this lower level

runners.name: Last Flight
runners.spotlight: Has not shown a lot against her own sex but she begins handicap life on a lowly mark and this longer trip has to help on pedigree; better can be expected for a yard among the winners.
runners.comment: Begins handicap life on a lowly mark and this longer trip has to help on pedigree

runners.name: Panthere Noir
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden who ran one of his better races last time, from the front on his first crack at beyond 1m2f; had any number of chances, however, and is vulnerable to improvers.
runners.comment: One of his better runs last time but had any number of chances and vulnerable to improvers

runners.name: Ada Rose
runners.spotlight: Better effort from the front over C&D in April but failed to build on that when beaten a long way on her turf debut (behind Keep It Classic) last time; some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on her turf debut last time; some way down the pecking order

runners.name: Al Amirah
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden who's run poorly twice since handicapping, latterly over C&D early last month; hard to make a case for her.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden who's run poorly since handicapping, latterly over C&D early last month

runners.name: Ethereal Charm
runners.spotlight: Seemingly exposed as ordinary, the step up to 1m2f not making any difference last time; is a half-sister to a couple of winning stayers, but that's rather clutching at straws.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a couple of winning stayers, but that's rather clutching at straws

runners.name: Bumaan
runners.spotlight: Out of a sister to Oaks/King George winner Taghrooda and this much stiffer test should help as he goes handicapping at a lowly level in a first-time tongue-tie; showed little in three Polytrack runs in the spring though (7f-1m); market informative.
runners.comment: Stiffer test should help as he goes handicapping at lowly level in a first-time tongue-tie

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes

runners.name: Arlecchino's Rex
runners.spotlight: Course regular who's proved very consistent since the hood went back on; usually held up and wants a good test over this trip but he is more likely than most at this level to give his running, dropping back down from 0-60 company; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Course regular who's proved very consistent since the hood went back on; each-way shout

runners.name: First Encounter
runners.spotlight: Serial hard-puller who won a 0-50 over C&D in the winter and has held his form well; not beaten far in handicaps on last two outings and may have needed it last time after a break; one to consider.
runners.comment: Serial hard-puller who won a 0-50 over C&D in the winter and has held his form well

runners.name: Taskheer
runners.spotlight: Hard-puller who ran below par in first-time cheekpieces (off tonight) last time; previously consistent at this sort of level but will need to settle better back up in trip after a six-week break.
runners.comment: Consistent at this level but needs to settle better back up in trip after a six-week break

runners.name: Smoker Bellamy
runners.spotlight: Clear second to a well-treated winner on comeback here in April but nowhere near meeting market expectations in three other handicaps; this race is easier, though, for a yard that ended a dry run with two jump winners last Friday.
runners.comment: Clear 2nd to a well-treated winner on comeback here in April; player if back to that level

runners.name: Sorted
runners.spotlight: Nothing wrong with his C&D handicap second in March and his subsequent third here at a short price is best overlooked; open to a bit of improvement still and is one of those best placed to take advantage should Rockafeller Skank not fire.
runners.comment: Nothing wrong with his C&D handicap second in March; open to a bit of improvement still

runners.name: Manton Road
runners.spotlight: Progressive in the early months of last year for Gay Kelleway but has lost his way; runs in 0-50 company for the first time but that's as a consequence of having regressed further for his current yard this season.
runners.comment: Runs in a 0-50 for the first time but that's as a consequence of having regressed further

runners.name: Saturnalia
runners.spotlight: Couple of absences in recent times and he has regressed (well drawn when faring better in a soft-ground handicap last time); goes well here, though, and holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Couple of absences in recent times and regressed; goes well here though; each-way claims
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Thomas Tallis
runners.spotlight: Poor in three runs last year before gelded; again started a big price and didn't achieve much in a similar race over C&D on his comeback a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Big price and didn't achieve much in a similar race on his C&D comeback a fortnight ago

runners.name: Gloriola
runners.spotlight: Yet to get involved after six starts and looked to find 1m stretching her stamina in two runs in the spring; wide stall won't help and she needs to improve for the switch to the AW.
runners.comment: Looked to find 1m stretching her stamina in two runs in the spring; wide stall won't help

runners.name: Vecchio
runners.spotlight: Longstanding, poor maiden who hasn't finished placed in a long time; will do well to get involved.
runners.comment: Longstanding, poor maiden who hasn't finished placed in ages; will do well to get involved

runners.name: Rockafeller Skank
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in three runs last backend before gelded; different proposition now, winning two classifieds at Yarmouth (soft) in the space of 24 hours last midweek; they were two weak 0-50s and he has a double penalty to overcome but he looks better than this level.
runners.comment: Two easy wins at Yarmouth last week; looks better than this level and the one to beat

runners.name: Rogue Soldier
runners.spotlight: Sharply regressive for this yard, having missed nine months, and finished last in six of his last seven runs; latest win came in cheekpieces and today's first-time blinkers will need to make a huge difference.
runners.comment: Sharply regressive for this yard, having missed nine months; big shock to see him win

races.race_name: Book To Stay At Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Argy Bhaji
runners.spotlight: Now 0-5 but has yet to run a bad race and has been knocking on the door in 6f handicaps; solid claims.
runners.comment: Now 0-5 but has yet to run a bad race and has been knocking on the door in 6f handicaps

runners.name: Spaceman
runners.spotlight: Solid debut when dead-heating for second at Redcar last November (6f) and probably showed similar form when a beaten favourite at Newcastle (5f); just about sets the standard here.
runners.comment: Placed over 6f (good) and 5f (AW); just about sets the standard here

runners.name: Project Wallace
runners.spotlight: Gelded 75,000euros yearling; 22-1 at Newcastle (5f) but ran well from the front, finishing just behind Spaceman; improvement looks on the cards.
runners.comment: 22-1 at Newcastle (5f) but ran well from the front, finishing just behind Spaceman

runners.name: Good Guys Girl
runners.spotlight: £21,000 yearling; tenth foal; half-sister to winners Go On Go On Go On (5f/6f; RPR 100), Dahawi (5f/6f; 97), Ronnie The Rooster (7f/1m; 71) and Piranha Rama (6f 2yo; 65); dam 5f AW 2yo winner (80); lacks experience but bred to go on the surface.
runners.comment: £21,000 yearling; lacks experience but bred to go on the surface
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing & Alfa Site Services

runners.name: Baby G
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to 6f AW winner Ok Pal (RPR 54); dam placed 6f/7.3f 2yo (62), out of maiden half-sister to Swedish 1m5f Listed winner Account Express; best watched on this belated debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 6f AW winner Ok Pal (RPR 54); best watched on this belated debut

runners.name: Moonlight Melody
runners.spotlight: Beaten 7l when fifth of the six runners on good ground at Catterick (6f); she wouldn't be the obvious answer.
runners.comment: Beaten 7l when fifth of the six runners on good ground at Catterick (6f)

runners.name: Roy Lane
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard when last of six on his debut last May and hung left when beaten a long way on his comeback, having been gelded; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: In a top yard but very opposable on what he's shown so far; tries cheekpieces
runners.owner: Mrs Polly Gibson & Miss Pollyanne Gibson

runners.name: Sheik Yerbouti
runners.spotlight: Well down the field in both his races, at odds of 150-1 and 200-1; no appeal for now.
runners.comment: Well down the field in both his races, at odds of 150-1 and 200-1; no appeal for now

runners.name: Beachborough Girl
runners.spotlight: Ultra-consistent in handicaps, aside from her turf efforts, and showed the right attitude back in cheekpieces when narrowly beating Study Up over C&D two weeks ago; both will find this a bit tougher but she has claims upped 3lb.
runners.comment: Ultra-consistent in handicaps, aside from her turf efforts; claims once more upped 3lb

runners.name: Study Up
runners.spotlight: Scrapped it out with Beachborough Girl over C&D a fortnight ago, on his first start without a hood, and closely matched with that one on revised terms; got caught wide early that night from stall 12 and looks set to go well once more.
runners.comment: Scrapped it out with Beachborough Girl over C&D a fortnight ago; on the shortlist

runners.name: Eagle Day
runners.spotlight: Wildly inconsistent these days but boasts a decent record round here (five wins from 19 starts); back around the mark off which he won here in March (awarded the race) and he is quite capable of bouncing back from two poor efforts on turf.
runners.comment: Wildly inconsistent these days but boasts a decent record round here; could bounce back

runners.name: Asian Journey
runners.spotlight: String of defeats at short prices before finally off the mark over 8.6f here a fortnight ago off a 2lb lower mark in 0-60 company; has not always looked the easiest and has to prove his stamina going into a deeper race.
runners.comment: Has not always looked the easiest; needs to prove his stamina upped 2lb into a deeper race

runners.name: Pleasant Man
runners.spotlight: Not as good now and has become hard to win with; on a competitive mark again but stays a good bit further than this and others make more obvious appeal.
runners.comment: On a competitive mark again but stays a good bit further; others look stronger

runners.name: Wolf Of Badenoch
runners.spotlight: Second in a Group 2 for Hugo Palmer as a 2yo before proving hard to place last year; found his level again but twice turned over at short prices recently (latest third has been boosted since) and has stamina to prove upped from 1m back on the AW.
runners.comment: Twice turned over at short prices recently (latest third boosted) and has stamina to prove

runners.name: Have You A Minute
runners.spotlight: Two wins and two seconds from six runs over C&D but he finished last season on a downswing and there's nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh; used to plying his trade at a lower level too.
runners.comment: Finished last season on a downswing and there's nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh

runners.name: Le Pelerin
runners.spotlight: Regressive, 13-race maiden who hasn't improved since undergoing wind surgery during the winter; twice ran well off higher marks over C&D last autumn, so a case can be made, but no upturn with the tongue-tie enlisted on latest outing and he's now given a go in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Twice ran well off higher marks over C&D last autumn; given a go in first-time headgear

runners.name: Kaaranah
runners.spotlight: Currently 2-2 over C&D and 2-2 in cheekpieces (again off tonight) following wins here in April; excuses off his revised mark since and he is one to consider back on Tapeta.
runners.comment: Currently 2-2 over C&D and 2-2 in cheekpieces (again off tonight); one to consider

runners.name: Saliko
runners.spotlight: Dual Nottingham winner last summer, latterly off a 2lb higher mark; partial revival in first-time cheekpieces latest but was allowed her own way in front and she'll need to find more again.
runners.comment: Partial revival in first-time cheekpieces latest but she was allowed her own way in front

runners.name: Implied Volatility
runners.spotlight: Yet  to finish placed after nine starts (6f-1m2f); bit of support here when last seen but he ran poorly and is some way down the pecking order back from five months off.
runners.comment: Bit of support here when last seen but ran poorly and is some way down the pecking order

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino Hull Dine In Style From £35 EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Persian Land
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut at Haydock (6f, soft) in May and again rallied when a neck second of five at Ffos Las (7.3f, good to soft) two weeks later; the stiff finish here should be in his favour and he should go well again in a first-time visor.
runners.comment: Rallied when winning at Haydock and second at Ffos Las; this stiff finish should suit him

runners.name: Waasil
runners.spotlight: No extra late on when fourth of six on debut at Hamilton (6f, good; 5-2 joint-favourite) last month; that form has taken a couple of knocks but this colt should still improve and the extra yardage ought to be within his range.
runners.comment: No extra late on when fourth of six on debut at Hamilton; should improve

runners.name: Out Of This World
runners.spotlight: Well backed on debut over C&D (good to firm; 2-1 favourite) three weeks ago and did well to win after getting upset in the stalls and starting slowly; open to plenty of improvement and could follow up.
runners.comment: Made winning debut here after getting upset in the stalls/starting slowly; could follow up

runners.name: Weleyff
runners.spotlight: 145,000euros foal, 145,000gns yearling; Too Darn Hot half-brother to French 1m5f winner Rouge; dam 7f AW winner (RPR 81), half-sister to useful 7f winner Tamadhor; changed hands for six figures as foal and yearling; the more interesting of the newcomers on paper.
runners.comment: Changed hands for six figures as foal and yearling; interesting newcomer on paper

runners.name: Rule Of The Agency
runners.spotlight: By Without Parole; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 76), half-sister to 7.4f 2yo/1m2f winner Doubletalk, out of Brazilian 1m2f Grade 2 winner; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: By Without Parole; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 76); worth a market check on debut

runners.name: High Hazard
runners.spotlight: Represents a stable whose 2yos have been going well, but he beat only one home at Thirsk (7f, good; 10-1) and needs a big leap forward.
runners.comment: Beat only one home at Thirsk and needs a big leap forward

runners.name: Fasthope
runners.spotlight: Got the hang of things late on when 7l third of nine at Pontefract (6f, good to soft; 50-1) last month, keeping on well after being unbalanced on the bend; has a fair bit to find on the figures but he should know more this time and the extra furlong should be in his favour.
runners.comment: Green when third at Pontefract; fair bit to find but should know more this time

runners.name: Fozzie
runners.spotlight: Belied 66-1 odds and showed fairly useful form when second on seasonal debut at Thirsk (7f, good) in April but was below that level when making the frame on next two outings, particularly so when well-held third of four at Pontefract (1m2f, good; raced too freely) a fortnight ago; this drop back in trip looks the right move but he still has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Second over 7f in April; well held over 1m2f last time; back in trip with point to prove

runners.name: Bee Farmer
runners.spotlight: Has a good pedigree and bettered his low-key debut when second (albeit no match for useful winner) at Redcar (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago; can progress again; likely contender.
runners.comment: Bettered low-key debut when second at Redcar last month, albeit no match for useful winner

runners.name: Race To The Edge
runners.spotlight: Displayed significant ability on both starts, more recently when keeping-on third of 11 at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) ten days ago; that form already makes him a big player here and he's open to improvement.
runners.comment: Kept on for third at Doncaster recently and open to more improvement here; big player

runners.name: Glen Nevis
runners.spotlight: Out of a well-connected mare and made promising debut when clear second to useful rival at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) in April; took backward step and finished behind Bee Farmer when well-held fourth at Redcar (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago but it would be reckless to write him off after that.
runners.comment: Well held when fourth last month but his debut second in April was promising
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Indefensible
runners.spotlight: The most exposed runner in this line-up, having raced six times, but came second in C&D handicap in April and filled same position in recent Thirsk maiden (7f, good); wears cheekpieces today; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Second in C&D handicap in April and also ran well in a recent maiden; won't be far away

runners.name: Melting Snow
runners.spotlight: Out of a very useful and well-connected 1m2f winner; bettered low-key debut when keeping on for never-dangerous fifth at Lingfield (7f, AW) last month and can probably improve again in due course but others in this field have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Second run was better than her debut but others appeal more all the same

runners.name: Lorca's Waltz
runners.spotlight: Out of an Australian Group 1 sprint winner but showed only minor promise in two AW novice races during the winter (8.6f/1m); gelded in February; makes turf debut with tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise in two AW novice races during the winter (8.6f/1m)
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Wats On Tiger
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; dam placed 1m3f/1m4f AW (RPR 71), out of unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 1 (Cheveley Park) winner Seazun; seems unlikely to meet the standard required on this belated debut.
runners.comment: Seems unlikely to meet the standard required on this belated debut

runners.name: Elias Escape
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a telling blow on first two starts (7f/6f) and will probably be of greater interest in run-of-the-mill handicaps.
runners.comment: Unable to land a telling blow on first two starts (7f/6f); handicaps beckon

runners.name: I Can Boogy
runners.spotlight: Has a record of 2-8 on AW compared to just 1-13 on turf and ran below par returned to this sphere last time; has produced best results this year in classified events.
runners.comment: Just 1-13 on turf and ran below par returned to this sphere last time

runners.name: Lope Y Linda
runners.spotlight: Has shown improvement in last two starts, winning under Jake Dickson at Catterick (7f) then second at Musselburgh (1m) behind a rival who was soon clear and not for catching; respected in current form.
runners.comment: Has shown improvement with win/second in last two starts; respected in current form

runners.name: Valsharah
runners.spotlight: Well treated on plenty of form but has been unable to take advantage of a steadily declining mark for current connections and latest defeat took his record to 0-24.
runners.comment: Maiden; unable to take advantage of declining mark for current connections

runners.name: Elettaria
runners.spotlight: Drops back in trip having failed to sustain her effort over 8.5f at Beverley last time; has plenty of C&D form, including third-place finish in this race last year; could go well.
runners.comment: Has plenty of C&D form, including third-place finish in this race last year

runners.name: Hear The Drums
runners.spotlight: Clear best form when winning at Leicester (7f) on final 2yo start and sole attempt on ground softer than good; only interesting if such conditions materialise.
runners.comment: Clear best form when winning at Leicester on sole start on softer than good

runners.name: Emerald Army
runners.spotlight: Three wins for previous yard; has encouraging form this term for new stable and last two efforts (runner-up over 7f both times) suggest his turn is near; 3lb below last winning mark; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Last two efforts suggest his turn is near; 3lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Oselton
runners.spotlight: Registered sole win on AW but he produced a career-best RPR when going down by only a neck at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) most recently; possibilities, provided that form is backed up.
runners.comment: AW winner; produced a career-best RPR when close second on turf last time; possibilities

runners.name: Crystal Aurora
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent maiden but produced one of her better efforts when fourth at Ripon (1m) last time; again has frame possibilities, provided she manages to stay in similar form.
runners.comment: Produced one her better efforts when fourth at Ripon last time

runners.name: Miss Willows
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a success; however, two duck eggs this term help to reflect her inconsistency and leave a big doubt over her current form to boot.
runners.comment: Two duck eggs this term leave a big doubt over her current form

runners.name: Billyjoegold
runners.spotlight: Holds very weak claims on his AW form; chance depends on the effects of switching to turf.
runners.comment: Holds very weak claims on his AW form

runners.name: Indy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Recorded sole success over 6f on AW; has poor claims on 2026 form and folded tamely upped to 7f most recently; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Has poor claims on 2026 form

runners.name: Summer Rain
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame a few times over sprint trips on Wolverhampton AW; well held over 7f on stable/seasonal debut, exacerbating a turf record that is far from solid.
runners.comment: Maiden whose turf record remains far from solid

runners.name: Langholm
runners.spotlight: Veteran who hasn't yet found his form this term and hasn't won a handicap for nearly two years; others preferred.
runners.comment: Veteran who hasn't yet found his form this term

runners.name: Bantz
runners.spotlight: Scored twice in the first third of 2025 but current mark reflects a deterioration of his form, latterly in both starts this term for new stable.
runners.comment: Current mark reflects a deterioration in his form

runners.name: Jojo Rabbit
runners.spotlight: Never the slightest threat at Wolverhampton last time but there's nothing wrong with his two previous turf runs which included an unlucky second here; big player off the same mark if on a going day.
runners.comment: Last two turf runs include unlucky second here; big player off same mark if on a going day
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing XIII & Ruth Carr

runners.name: Ay Gee Ell
runners.spotlight: Blitzed his rivals at Southwell in December (5f) when a visor was first used; mostly creditable efforts since until down the field at Pontefract on latest; needs to bounce back from that.
runners.comment: Mostly creditable efforts until down the field at Pontefract; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Bibendum
runners.spotlight: Stayed on well to win at Newcastle in November but hasn't threatened since, off four months before latest run; drawn widest; others preferred.
runners.comment: Stayed on well to win at Newcastle in November but no real threat since; others preferred

runners.name: Miss Brazen
runners.spotlight: Made winning return under Lewis Chalkley at Nottingham (5f, good) in April and has remained in form, better than the bare result suggests when sixth of seven at Musselburgh (very slowly away) 11 days ago; in the mix.
runners.comment: Made winning return at Nottingham in April and has remained in form; in the mix

runners.name: Dream Deal
runners.spotlight: All three wins at Catterick (has never run here before); below par both starts this season, but last month's sixth at Redcar was a clear step forward and he's not ruled out from a falling mark.
runners.comment: Last month's sixth at Redcar was a clear step forward; not ruled out from a falling mark

runners.name: Keldeo
runners.spotlight: Notched her third Catterick win (6f, good) in April, since when she's been a bit below par with three fourth places; handles the 5f course here but needs a revival.
runners.comment: Below par three times since April Catterick win; needs a revival
runners.owner: Ownaracehorse, O Pears and T&T Racing Ltd

runners.name: Lord Abama
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who made an encouraging reappearance at Thirsk in April; hasn't built on it, but this drop to 5f (for just the third time in a long career) is an interesting move and the fitting of a tongue-strap might help too; well drawn on the rail.
runners.comment: Front-runner; drop to 5f and fitting of tongue-tie could spark return to form; interesting
runners.owner: Mrs K S Pratt & M Dods

runners.name: Azuinthejungle
runners.spotlight: Rattled off a hat-trick last summer, including over C&D, and he's now just 1lb higher than for the last of those wins; shaped as if coming to the boil when fourth at Ripon two weeks ago; versatile in terms of ground and looks ready to strike.
runners.comment: Three wins last summer; shaped as if coming to the boil last time; looks ready to strike
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Fierce
runners.spotlight: Came good twice last summer and he's knocking at the door again, doing his best work late on when second at Carlisle (5f, good) four weeks ago; one of the likelier candidates.
runners.comment: Came good twice last summer and knocking at the door again; one of the likelier candidates

runners.name: Spirit Of Applause
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who was in good form last autumn; the booking of the promising Jake Dickson catches the eye, but he's been off for over seven months and doesn't have a great record when fresh.
runners.comment: C&D winner; returns from over seven months off and doesn't have a great record when fresh

runners.name: Rogers Dream
runners.spotlight: Maiden who posted an improved effort in 8.5f AW classified contest this month on stable debut and return from lengthy absence; needs another step forward to get off the mark.
runners.comment: Improved effort in AW classified this month on stable debut and return from layoff

runners.name: Hostelry
runners.spotlight: 9yo mare whose C&D form includes one of her five wins last term and a resurgent effort on penultimate start; latest race didn't pan out favourably; runner-up in this contest three years ago; likely player.
runners.comment: C&D form includes one of her five wins last term and resurgent effort on penultimate run

runners.name: Copper And Five
runners.spotlight: Veteran whose recent consistent vein of form has taken his C&D record to 14224, latest effort in the race won by Anificas Beauty; remains 4lb below last wining mark; holds solid claims.
runners.comment: Recent consistent vein of form has taken his C&D record to 14224; solid claims
runners.owner: Mrs Sally Doyle And Mrs Ruth Carr

runners.name: Can I Kiss You
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent maiden in Ireland for James Barrett; faded into midfield last time, failing to back up her reappearance effort; makes debut for new yard.
runners.comment: Inconsistent maiden in Ireland; makes debut for new yard

runners.name: Little Ted
runners.spotlight: Losing run is mounting up and he didn't look particularly resolute when nevertheless going close at Musselburgh (1m) most recently; chance depends on the effects of new headgear.
runners.comment: Didn't look particularly resolute when nevertheless going close last time; new headgear

runners.name: Wicklow Way
runners.spotlight: Record remains far from solid and he has something to prove on a few counts; showed best form when winning 1m2f AW classified event in March.
runners.comment: Showed clear best form when winning 1m2f AW classified event in March

runners.name: Mavetheforcebewivu
runners.spotlight: Made the frame a few times last summer; broadly not in the same form since and remains a maiden.
runners.comment: Has a doubt over current form and remains a maiden

runners.name: Anificas Beauty
runners.spotlight: Proved resurgent with 66-1 success in similar event over C&D (good to firm) last time and has it in her to follow up, having gained back-to-back wins on two occasions in the past; respected.
runners.comment: Resurgent with C&D success last time; has twice gained back-to-back wins in the past

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Hull Handicap

runners.name: Inspiring Speeches
runners.spotlight: Back-to-wins last summer, over C&D (soft) and on good ground at Catterick; off the same mark as for that latest win and returned to form with a close fifth last time (went from the front).
runners.comment: Off the same mark as for latest win and returned to form with a close fifth last time

runners.name: Quirke On Parole
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last June but has been very quiet in her two runs this season; best watched until showing some spark.
runners.comment: C&D winner last June but has been very quiet in her two runs this season
runners.owner: A Quirke & Mrs G B Walford

runners.name: Hijo De La Luna
runners.spotlight: Won a classified race at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in February in a first-time visor; from the front, he was just behind Dandy's Angel at Hamilton last time; must have some chance.
runners.comment: From the front, he was just behind Dandy's Angel at Hamilton last time

runners.name: Dandy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner who loves it here and there were signs of a resurgence when third at Hamilton (beaten 4.5l); considered.
runners.comment: Loves it here and there were signs of a resurgence when third at Hamilton

runners.name: Regal Glory
runners.spotlight: Only 1-39 and last two runs over 2m and 1m4f were underwhelming; did run quite well in two starts during April so can't be dismissed; 1lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Only 1-39 and last two runs over 2m and 1m4f were underwhelming; ran well in April

runners.name: Daring Leader
runners.spotlight: Has won over 1m on turf (good) and 1m2f on the AW; needs to rediscover his best form and latest third was quite remote.
runners.comment: Dual winner but needs to rediscover his best form and latest third was quite remote
runners.owner: D J Jones & M T Brougham

runners.name: Sassy Glory
runners.spotlight: AW winner over 1m but 1-29 overall so now fully exposed; well behind Inspiring Speeches last time.
runners.comment: 1-29 overall so now fully exposed; well behind Inspiring Speeches last time
runners.owner: Ursa Major & Mark Walford Racing Club

runners.name: Sweet Cicely
runners.spotlight: Has the odd bit of competitive form but very quiet of late and now 0-20; tries blinkers; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Has the odd bit of competitive form but very quiet of late and now 0-20; tries blinkers

runners.name: Rajawail
runners.spotlight: 1lb out of the weights and this longstanding maiden hasn't been remotely threatening this season.
runners.comment: This longstanding maiden hasn't been remotely threatening this season

runners.name: Pureis King
runners.spotlight: Hasn't offered a lot in races at about 1m but this bumper winner can fare better now handicapping at a low level.
runners.comment: This bumper winner can fare better now handicapping at a low level

runners.name: Pisanello
runners.spotlight: Has gone 0-24 since winning over 1m1f at York (good to soft) two years ago and needs to improve on this season's efforts.
runners.comment: On a losing run of 24 and needs to improve on this season's efforts
